info.title: Meetings
info.description: The Zoom Meeting webhooks allow developers to receive events for Zoom Meetings and Webinars.

requestBody.description:
# meeting.participant_jbh_waiting

The **Participant waiting for host to join** event is triggered every time an attendee has been waiting for the host to join the meeting for at least 30 seconds. The host of the meeting must be a user in your account or users in any other accounts that have installed your [webhook-enabled](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#enable-webhooks) app.

When scheduling a meeting, the organizer or host of the meeting can enable the **Enable join before host** option. This allows attendees to join the meeting before the host of the meeting arrives. This meeting also starts as soon as the first meeting participant joins.

If this option is **disabled**, participants are prompted to wait for the host to arrive before joining the meeting.

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or a higher plan.
* **Disable** the **Enable join before host** option for the scheduled meeting.
* The attendee has been waiting for at least 30 seconds before the event is triggered.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Participant waiting for host to join** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.


**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant:master`,`meeting:read:participant`,`meeting:read:participant:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.participant_jbh_waiting`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (personal audio conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

customer_key.description: The participant's SDK identifier. This value can be alphanumeric.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (personal audio conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

customer_key.description: The participant's SDK identifier. This value can be alphanumeric.

# meeting.summary_recovered

The **Meeting summary has been recovered from trash** event is triggered every time one of your app users or account users recovers a summary from the trash.

## Prerequisites

* Host user must be Pro or higher plan.
* Enable the **Meeting Summary with AI Companion** feature in the host's account.
* E2ee meetings do not have summary feature enabled.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Meeting summary has been recovered from trash** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting_summary:read:admin`,`meeting_summary:read`,`meeting_summary:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:summary:master`,`meeting:read:summary`,`meeting:read:summary:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.summary_recovered`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who hosted the meeting.

operator.description: The email address of the user who recovered the meeting summary.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who recovered the meeting summary.

meeting_host_id.description: The ID of the user who is set as the meeting host.

meeting_uuid.description: Unique meeting ID. Each meeting instance generates its own meeting UUID - after a meeting ends, a new UUID is generated for the next instance of the meeting. Retrieve a list of UUIDs from past meeting instances using the [**List past meeting instances**](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/#operation/pastMeetings) API. [Double encode](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#meeting-id-and-uuid) your UUID when using it for API calls if the UUID begins with a / or contains // in it.

meeting_id.description: [Meeting ID](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362373-What-is-a-Meeting-ID-) - the meeting's unique identifier in **long** format, represented as int64 data type in JSON. Also known as the meeting number.

meeting_start_time.description: The meeting's start date and time.

meeting_end_time.description: The meeting's end date and time.

summary_start_time.description: The summary's start date and time.

summary_end_time.description: The summary's end date and time.

summary_created_time.description: The date and time when the meeting summary was created.

summary_last_modified_time.description: The date and time when the meeting summary was last modified.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who hosted the meeting.

operator.description: The email address of the user who recovered the meeting summary.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who recovered the meeting summary.

meeting_host_id.description: The ID of the user who is set as the meeting host.

meeting_uuid.description: Unique meeting ID. Each meeting instance generates its own meeting UUID - after a meeting ends, a new UUID is generated for the next instance of the meeting. Retrieve a list of UUIDs from past meeting instances using the [**List past meeting instances**](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/#operation/pastMeetings) API. [Double encode](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#meeting-id-and-uuid) your UUID when using it for API calls if the UUID begins with a / or contains // in it.

meeting_id.description: [Meeting ID](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362373-What-is-a-Meeting-ID-) - the meeting's unique identifier in **long** format, represented as int64 data type in JSON. Also known as the meeting number.

meeting_start_time.description: The meeting's start date and time.

meeting_end_time.description: The meeting's end date and time.

summary_start_time.description: The summary's start date and time.

summary_end_time.description: The summary's end date and time.

summary_created_time.description: The date and time when the meeting summary was created.

summary_last_modified_time.description: The date and time when the meeting summary was last modified.

# meeting.participant_left_breakout_room

The **Meeting participant has left a breakout room** event is triggered when a meeting participant leaves a meeting [breakout room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115005769646-Participating-in-breakout-rooms).

Breakout rooms are sessions that are split off from the main Zoom meeting. They let participants meet in smaller groups and are completely isolated in terms of audio and video from the main session.

## Prerequisites

* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Meeting participant has left a breakout room** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant:master`,`meeting:read:participant`,`meeting:read:participant:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.participant_left_breakout_room`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The main meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

breakout_room_uuid.description: The breakout room's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each breakout room instance generates a breakout room UUID.

host_id.description: The main meeting host's user ID.

type.description:
The main meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

user_id.description: The participant's meeting user ID. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

parent_user_id.description: The participant's main meeting user ID (user_id).

id.description:
The participant's unique ID. 
* If meeting registration was **not** required and the participant joined by logging into Zoom, this value is the same as the userId field used in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API. 
* If registration was required for the meeting and the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the same as the id value in the [**List meeting registrants**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/meetingRegistrants) API response. 
* If participant joins without logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value. 

 This value returns blank for external users.

**Note:** Use the participant_user_id value instead of this value. We will remove this response in a future release.

participant_uuid.description: The participant's UUID for this specific meeting and any breakout rooms created in this meeting. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of that meeting.

leave_time.description: The time when the participant left the meeting.

leave_reason.description: The reason why the participant left the meeting.

email.description: The participant's email address. This response only returns if the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom.

registrant_id.description: The participant's registrant ID. A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026).

participant_user_id.description:
The participant's unique ID. 
* If meeting registration was **not** required and the participant joined by logging into Zoom, this value is the same as the userId field used in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API. 
* If registration was required for the meeting and the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the same as the id value in the [**List meeting registrants**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/meetingRegistrants) API response. 
* If participant joins without logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

This value returns blank for external users.

phone_number.description: Phone number of participant joined via PSTN.

customer_key.description: The participant's SDK identifier. This value can be alphanumeric.

json-example.value:
{ "event": "meeting.participant_left_breakout_room", "event_ts": 1626230691572, "payload": { "account_id": "AAAAAABBBB", "object": { "id": "1234567890", "uuid": "4444AAAiAAAAAiAiAiiAii==", "breakout_room_uuid": "FkQbpP2UR028mDrwzEahqw==", "host_id": "x1yCzABCDEfg23HiJKl4mN", "topic": "My Meeting", "type": 8, "start_time": "2021-07-13T21:44:51Z", "timezone": "America/Los_Angeles", "duration": 60, "participant": { "user_id": "31228928", "parent_user_id": "1234567890", "user_name": "Jill Chill", "id": "iFxeBPYun6SAiWUzBcEkX", "participant_uuid": "55555AAAiAAAAAiAiAiiAii", "leave_time": "2021-07-13T22:50:51Z", "leave_reason": "Jill Chill left the meeting.
Reason: Host ended the meeting.", "date_time": "2021-07-13T21:44:51Z", "email": "jchill@example.com", "registrant_id": "abcdefghij0-klmnopq23456", "participant_user_id": "rstuvwxyza789-cde", "phone_number": "8615250064084", "customer_key": "349589LkJyeW" } } } }

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The main meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

breakout_room_uuid.description: The breakout room's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each breakout room instance generates a breakout room UUID.

host_id.description: The main meeting host's user ID.

type.description:
The main meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

user_id.description: The participant's meeting user ID. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

parent_user_id.description: The participant's main meeting user ID (user_id).

id.description:
The participant's unique ID. 
* If meeting registration was **not** required and the participant joined by logging into Zoom, this value is the same as the userId field used in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API. 
* If registration was required for the meeting and the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the same as the id value in the [**List meeting registrants**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/meetingRegistrants) API response. 
* If participant joins without logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value. 

 This value returns blank for external users.

**Note:** Use the participant_user_id value instead of this value. We will remove this response in a future release.

participant_uuid.description: The participant's UUID for this specific meeting and any breakout rooms created in this meeting. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of that meeting.

leave_time.description: The time when the participant left the meeting.

leave_reason.description: The reason why the participant left the meeting.

email.description: The participant's email address. This response only returns if the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom.

registrant_id.description: The participant's registrant ID. A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026).

participant_user_id.description:
The participant's unique ID. 
* If meeting registration was **not** required and the participant joined by logging into Zoom, this value is the same as the userId field used in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API. 
* If registration was required for the meeting and the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the same as the id value in the [**List meeting registrants**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/meetingRegistrants) API response. 
* If participant joins without logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

This value returns blank for external users.

phone_number.description: Phone number of participant joined via PSTN.

customer_key.description: The participant's SDK identifier. This value can be alphanumeric.

json-example.value:
{ "event": "meeting.participant_left_breakout_room", "event_ts": 1626230691572, "payload": { "account_id": "AAAAAABBBB", "object": { "id": "1234567890", "uuid": "4444AAAiAAAAAiAiAiiAii==", "breakout_room_uuid": "FkQbpP2UR028mDrwzEahqw==", "host_id": "x1yCzABCDEfg23HiJKl4mN", "topic": "My Meeting", "type": 8, "start_time": "2021-07-13T21:44:51Z", "timezone": "America/Los_Angeles", "duration": 60, "participant": { "user_id": "31228928", "parent_user_id": "1234567890", "user_name": "Jill Chill", "id": "iFxeBPYun6SAiWUzBcEkX", "participant_uuid": "55555AAAiAAAAAiAiAiiAii", "leave_time": "2021-07-13T22:50:51Z", "leave_reason": "Jill Chill left the meeting.
Reason: Host ended the meeting.", "date_time": "2021-07-13T21:44:51Z", "email": "jchill@example.com", "registrant_id": "abcdefghij0-klmnopq23456", "participant_user_id": "rstuvwxyza789-cde", "phone_number": "8615250064084", "customer_key": "349589LkJyeW" } } } }

# meeting.device_tested

The **Meeting Device Tested** event is triggered every time a user completes a [connected device test](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362283-Testing-computer-or-device-audio).

When a user joins a meeting, they choose to test their connected camera, microphone, and speaker devices. The Zoom client performs a test for the selected device and prompts a user to confirm whether the device works.

## Prerequisites

* Create the meeting via the [Create a Meeting](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/meetings/#tag/meetings/POST/users/{userId}/meetings) API with the `device_testing` set to true.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Meeting Device Tested** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:device:master`,`meeting:read:device`,`meeting:read:device:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.device_tested`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

test_result.description: Information about the meeting's device test results.

user_id.description: The user's ID.

camera_status.description:
The camera's status: 
* 2 — Device working. 
* 1 — Device not working. 
* 0 — Device not tested.

speaker_status.description:
The speaker's status: 
* 2 — Device working. 
* 1 — Device not working. 
* 0 — Device not tested.

microphone_status.description:
The microphone's status: 
* 2 — Device working. 
* 1 — Device not working. 
* 0 — Device not tested.

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

test_result.description: Information about the meeting's device test results.

user_id.description: The user's ID.

camera_status.description:
The camera's status: 
* 2 — Device working. 
* 1 — Device not working. 
* 0 — Device not tested.

speaker_status.description:
The speaker's status: 
* 2 — Device working. 
* 1 — Device not working. 
* 0 — Device not tested.

microphone_status.description:
The microphone's status: 
* 2 — Device working. 
* 1 — Device not working. 
* 0 — Device not tested.

# meeting.summary_shared

The **Meeting summary has been shared** event is triggered every time one of your app users or account users shares a meeting summary.

## Prerequisites

* Host user must have a Pro or higher plan.
* Enable the **Meeting Summary with AI Companion** feature in the host's account.
* E2ee meetings do not have summary feature enabled.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Meeting summary has been shared** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting_summary:read:admin`,`meeting_summary:read`,`meeting_summary:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:summary:master`,`meeting:read:summary`,`meeting:read:summary:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.summary_shared`

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who hosted the meeting.

operator.description: The email address of the user who shared the meeting summary.

# webinar.chat_message_file_downloaded
The **In-webinar chat message file has been downloaded** event is triggered when a message file from a webinar is downloaded.
The webhook is needed to enable the DLP option in the web portal.

## Prerequisites

* Have Zoom enable the DLP for in-meeting chat for your account.  This feature is **only** enabled upon request. Contact the [Zoom support team](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362003) for more information.
* Enable the **In-webinar chat message file has been downloaded** subscription for your app. In your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) go to **Event Subscriptions** > **Webinar**.


**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:admin`,`webinar:write:admin`,`webinar:read`,`webinar:write`,`webinar:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:chat_message`,`webinar:read:chat_message:admin`

**Event type**: `webinar.chat_message_file_downloaded`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who downloaded the file. This value returns blank for external users.

operator.description: The email address of the user who downloaded the file. This value returns the operator name for external users.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who downloaded the file. This value returns blank for external users.

object.description: Information about the chat message file.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a meeting UUID.

host_account_id.description: The webinar host's account ID. The value will be an empty string if this info is from an external user.

chat_message_file.description: Information about the chat message file.

file_id.description: The live webinar chat file's UUID.

file_name.description: The live webinar chat file's name.

file_size.description: The live webinar chat file's size, in bytes.

file_type.description: The live webinar chat file's type.

file_owner_id.description: The user ID of the user who uploaded the team chat file. This value returns blank for external users.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who downloaded the file. This value returns blank for external users.

operator.description: The email address of the user who downloaded the file. This value returns the operator name for external users.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who downloaded the file. This value returns blank for external users.

object.description: Information about the chat message file.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a meeting UUID.

host_account_id.description: The webinar host's account ID. The value will be an empty string if this info is from an external user.

chat_message_file.description: Information about the chat message file.

file_id.description: The live webinar chat file's UUID.

file_name.description: The live webinar chat file's name.

file_size.description: The live webinar chat file's size, in bytes.

file_type.description: The live webinar chat file's type.

# webinar.deleted

The **Webinar has been deleted** event is triggered every time a webinar scheduled by one of your app users or account users, is deleted.

A webinar host or a user with admin privileges can delete a scheduled webinar. Users can delete a webinar using the **Delete** feature for a webinar, from the [Zoom Web Portal](https://zoom.us/webinar/list) or the Zoom Client App. Alternatively, users can use the [**Delete a webinar**](/docs/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/webinarDelete) API to delete webinars programmatically.

## **Prerequisites**:
* Pro or a higher plan with Webinar license.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Webinar has been deleted** subscription enabled under the **Webinar** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:admin`,`webinar:write:admin`,`webinar:read`,`webinar:write`,`webinar:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:webinar:master`,`webinar:read:webinar`,`webinar:read:webinar:admin`

**Event type**: `webinar.deleted`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who deleted the webinar.

operator.description: The email address of the user who deleted the webinar.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who deleted the webinar.

operation.description:
The type of operation performed: 
* all — The change was applied to all webinars. 
* single — The change was applied only to a single webinar.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

type.description:
The webinar type: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

occurrences.description: Information about recurring webinars with fixed times and their occurrences. This only returns when the webinar's type value is 9.

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who deleted the webinar.

operator.description: The email address of the user who deleted the webinar.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who deleted the webinar.

The type of operation performed: 
* all — The change was applied to all webinars. 
* single — The change was applied only to a single webinar.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

The webinar type: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

occurrences.description: Information about recurring webinars with fixed times and their occurrences. This only returns when the webinar's type value is 9.

# recording.registration_approved

The **View registrant has approved for this recording** event is triggered every time a registrant is approved to view a cloud recording.

[Cloud recordings](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203741855-Cloud-Recording) of Zoom meetings and webinars can be made [on-demand](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360000488283-On-demand-Recordings). With on-demand recordings, users can view the recordings of past meetings and webinars at any time as long as they have not been removed. To view these recordings, users can register manually by filling out the registration form that pops up when users click on the **Recording URL**. After the registration, registrants that are approved will be able to view the recording.

An account owner, a host of the meeting/webinar or a user with admin privileges can customize the cloud recording [registration approval settings](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360000488283-On-demand-Recordings). If the **Automatically Approve** setting is enabled, registrants can view the recording automatically after a successful registration. If the **Manually Approve** setting is enabled, the registrants must be manually approved before they can view the recording.

Registrants can also be approved programmatically using the [**Update registrant status**](/api-reference/zoom-api/ma#operation/meetingRecordingRegistrantStatus) API.

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or higher plan.
* Cloud recording must be enabled on the users' account.
* The [approval setting](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360000488283-On-demand-Recordings) for the cloud recording registration must be set to **Manually Approve**.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **View registrant has approved for this recording** subscription enabled under the **Recording** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `recording:read:admin`,`recording:write:admin`,`recording:read`,`recording:write`,`recording:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `cloud_recording:read:registrant:master`,`cloud_recording:read:registrant`,`cloud_recording:read:registrant:admin`

**Event type**: `recording.registration_approved`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the host that recorded the meeting or webinar.

operator.description: The email address of the user that approved registration for the meeting or webinar recording.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user that approved registration for the meeting or webinar recording.

id.description: The ID of the recorded meeting (meetingId) or webinar (webinarId).

uuid.description: The universally unique identifier (UUID) of the recorded meeting or webinar instance.

host_id.description: The ID of the user set as the host of the meeting or webinar.

type.description:
The type of recorded meeting or webinar: 

 If the recording is of a meeting: 
* 1 — Instant meeting. 
* 2 — Scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A meeting created via PMI (Personal Meeting ID). 
* 7 — A [Personal Audio Conference](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204517069-Getting-Started-with-Personal-Audio-Conference) (PAC). 
* 8 - Recurring meeting with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is of a webinar: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is **not** from a meeting or webinar: 

* 99 — A recording uploaded via the [**Recordings**](https://zoom.us/recording) interface on the Zoom Web Portal.

start_time.description: The meeting or webinar's start time.

duration.description: The meeting or webinar's scheduled duration.

uuid.description: The universally unique identifier (UUID) of the recorded meeting or webinar instance.

 If the recording is of a meeting:

* 4 — A meeting created via PMI (Personal Meeting ID). 
* 7 — A [Personal Audio Conference](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204517069-Getting-Started-with-Personal-Audio-Conference) (PAC). 
* 8 - Recurring meeting with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is of a webinar:

* 99 — A recording uploaded via the [**Recordings**](https://zoom.us/recording) interface on the Zoom Web Portal.

start_time.description: The meeting or webinar's start time.

duration.description: The meeting or webinar's scheduled duration.

# meeting.risk_alert

The **At-Risk Meeting Notifier** event is triggered every time someone posts a Zoom meeting link to a social media account, such as Reddit or Twitter. The webhook sends the event to an authorized [OAuth app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#oauth-app).

The [**At-Risk Meeting Notifier (ARMN)**](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360052087972-At-Risk-Meeting-Notifier) scans public posts on social media sites and other public online resources for Zoom meeting links. When it finds publicly-posted meeting information indicating that a given meeting may be at high risk of being disrupted, Zoom notifies account owners and admins via email.

## Prerequisites

* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **At-Risk Meeting Notifier** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:risk_alert:master`,`meeting:read:risk_alert`,`meeting:read:risk_alert:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.risk_alert`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

* enableScreenShareHostOnly 
* enableSpecifiedDomain

* enableScreenShare 
* enableMultipleShare

# meeting.aic_transcript_completed

The **AIC transcript completed** triggers when the meeting transcript file is completed after a meeting or webinar ends.

## Prerequisites

* Enable the **Meeting Summary with AI Companion** feature and **Allow meeting hosts to retain and access meeting transcripts** in the host's account.
* Do not enable transcripts for E2ee meetings.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **AIC transcript completed** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting_summary:read`,`meeting_summary:read:admin`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:summary:admin`,`meeting:read:summary`

**Event type**: `meeting.aic_transcript_completed`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who hosted the meeting.

host_id.description: The ID of the user who is set as the meeting host.

meeting_uuid.description: Unique meeting ID. Each meeting instance generates its own meeting UUID - after a meeting ends, a new UUID is generated for the next instance of the meeting. Retrieve a list of UUIDs from past meeting instances using the [**List past meeting instances**](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/#operation/pastMeetings) API. [Double encode](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#meeting-id-and-uuid) your UUID when using it for API calls if the UUID begins with a / or contains // in it.

meeting_id.description: [Meeting ID](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362373-What-is-a-Meeting-ID-) - the meeting's unique identifier in **long** format, represented as int64 data type in JSON. Also known as the meeting number.

meeting_start_time.description: The meeting's start date and time.

attach_type.description: Each file ID has many attachments. Use this attach_type to download different attachments. In this webhook, the attach_type always equals durable_transcript.

host_id.description: The ID of the user who is set as the meeting host.

Retrieve a list of UUIDs from past meeting instances using the [**List past meeting instances**](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/#operation/pastMeetings) API. [Double encode](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#meeting-id-and-uuid) your UUID when using it for API calls if the UUID begins with a / or contains // in it.

meeting_start_time.description: The meeting's start date and time.

attach_type.description: Each file ID has many attachments. Use this attach_type to download different attachments. In this webhook, the attach_type always equals durable_transcript.

# webinar.participant_feedback

The **Get participant survey feedback for webinars** event is triggered every time an attendee completes an end-of-webinar [feedback survey](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115005855266-End-of-meeting-experience-feedback-survey) for a webinar. A webinar attendee is a webinar participant or the host.

## Prerequisites

Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
* The **Get participant survey feedback for webinars** subscription must be enabled under the **Webinar** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:admin`,`webinar:write:admin`,`webinar:read`,`webinar:write`,`webinar:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:participant_feedback:master`,`webinar:read:participant_feedback`,`webinar:read:participant_feedback:admin`

**Event type**: `webinar.participant_feedback`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who commits the feedback.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

participant_uuid.description: The participant's meeting universally unique ID (UUID). This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of the meeting. This value will not be changed when the participant joins/leaves a breakout room.

participant_user_id.description: The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). This value returns blank for external users.

feedback_details.description: The feedback details. This returns null if the satisfied field is true.

id.description:
The feedback ID. 
* 1 — Poor video quality. 
* 2 — Poor audio quality. 
* 5 — Unable to present using share.zoom.us. 
* 7 — Unable to make phone call. 
* 8 — They could not hear us. 
* 9 — We could not hear them. 
* 10 — They could not see us. 
* 11 — We could not see them. 
* 12 — Could not present computer screen. 
* 999 — Others.

comments.description: The participant's comments. This returns null if the satisfied field is true.

# meeting.participant_joined_waiting_room

The **Participant joined a waiting room** event is triggered every time an attendee joins a waiting room prior to being admitted to the meeting. The meeting host must be a user in your account or a user in any other accounts that have installed your [webhook-enabled](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/webhook-reference/#enable-webhooks) app.

The [**waiting room**](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115000332726-Waiting-Room) feature allows the host to control when participants join a meeting. When a participant attempts to join a meeting with this feature enabled, the participant is first admitted to the waiting room.
The meeting host can then admit attendees one by one or hold all attendees in the waiting room and admit them all at once.

## Prerequisites

* Enable the [**waiting room**](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115000332726-Waiting-Room) feature for the meeting.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Participant joined a waiting room** subscription must be enabled under the **Meeting** event.





**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant:master`,`meeting:read:participant`,`meeting:read:participant:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.participant_joined_waiting_room`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the main meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

participant.description: Information about the meeting participant. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, email returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

user_id.description: The participant's meeting user ID. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value. 

 This value returns blank for external users.

**Note:** Use the participant_user_id value instead of this value. We will remove this response in a future release.

participant_uuid.description: The participant's UUID for this specific meeting and any breakout rooms created in this meeting. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of that meeting.

date_time.description: The time when the participant joined the meeting.

email.description: The participant's email address. This response only returns if the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, this returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

phone_number.description: Phone number of participant joined via PSTN.

participant_user_id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

This value returns blank for external users.

registrant_id.description: The participant's registrant ID. A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026).

# meeting.ai_companion_assets_deleted
The **AI Companion assets deleted** event is triggered when all AI Companion meeting assets, such as transcripts and summaries, are deleted during a live meeting.

## Prerequisites

* Host user must be Pro or higher plan.
* Enable [AI Companion](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0057623).
* E2EE meetings do not support the AI Companion feature.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **AI Companion assets deleted** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read`,`meeting:read:admin`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:ai_companion`,`meeting:read:ai_companion:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.ai_companion_assets_deleted`

event_ts.description: The timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user that hosted the meeting.

object.description: The meeting where the AI Companion assets were deleted.

# webinar.converted_to_meeting
The **Webinar has been converted to a Meeting** event is triggered every time one of your app users or account users converts a webinar into a meeting.

## Prerequisites

* Pro or a higher plan with Webinar license.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Webinar has been converted to a Meeting** subscription enabled under the **Webinar** event.

# meeting.participant_jbh_joined

The **Participant joined meeting before host** event is triggered every time an attendee joins a meeting before the host. The host of the meeting must be a user in your account or a user in any other accounts that have installed your [webhook-enabled](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#enable-webhooks) app.

When scheduling a meeting, the organizer or host of the meeting can enable the **Enable join before host** option. This allows attendees to join the meeting before the host of the meeting arrives. This meeting also starts as soon as the first meeting participant joins.

If this option is **disabled**, participants are prompted to wait for the host to arrive before joining the meeting.

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or a higher plan.
* Enable the **Enable join before host** option for the scheduled meeting.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Participant joined meeting before host** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.





**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant:master`,`meeting:read:participant`,`meeting:read:participant:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.participant_jbh_joined`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (personal audio conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

customer_key.description: The participant's SDK identifier. This value can be alphanumeric.

registrant_id.description: The participant's registrant ID. A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026).

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

* 7 - A [PAC (personal audio conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

# meeting.invitation_accepted

The **Invited party answered a meeting invitation** event is triggered every time an invitation's recipient accepts an invitation to join a meeting or video call.
This event is only suitable for triggering with the ZOOM client.

## Prerequisites

* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with these configurations:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Invited party answered a meeting invitation** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.




**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

# recording.archive_files_completed

The **Recording Archive Files Completed** event is triggered every time the archived files of a meeting or webinar become available to download.

Zoom's [meeting and webinar archiving solution](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360050431572-Archiving-Meeting-and-Webinar-data) allows account administrators to set up an automated mechanism to collect and archive meeting data to a 3rd party platform of their choice. This helps to satisfy FINRA or other compliance requirements.

The archiving process begins once a meeting or webinar, hosted by an account that has enabled the archiving feature, begins. The archiving of the meeting data completes after a meeting or webinar ends.

This feature is **only** enabled upon request approval. Contact the [Zoom support team](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362003) for more information.

## Prerequisites

* The **Archiving** feature must be enabled on the user's account.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** in your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with these configurations.
  * A valid Event notification endpoint URL.
  * The **Recording Archive Files Completed** subscription enabled under the **Recording** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `recording:read:admin`,`recording:write:admin`,`recording:read`,`recording:write`,`recording:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `cloud_recording:read:archive_files:admin`

**Event type**: `recording.archive_files_completed`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

download_token.description:
Use the generated token value with the download_url value to download the cloud recording via an [OAuth app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/getting-started/app-types/create-oauth-app). The download token only lasts for 24 hours after its creation, and you can only download the file within 24 hours of receiving the event notification. You can either include the download_token as a query parameter or pass it as a Bearer token in the Authorization header of your HTTP request. 

 **Using an Authorization header (Recommended)** 

 
curl --request GET \
  --url {download_url} \
  --header 'authorization: Bearer {download_token} \
  --header 'content-type: application/json'
 

**Using a query parameter** 

 
{download_url}/?access_token={download_token}
 

 For example: https://zoom.us/recording/download/123456?access_token=abcdefgh

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who hosted the meeting or the webinar.

object.description: Information about the webinar or meeting recording.

uuid.description: The universally unique identifier (UUID) of the recorded meeting or webinar instance. Each meeting or webinar instance generates its own meeting UUID.

id.description: The ID of the meeting (meetingId) or the webinar (webinarId).

host_id.description: The ID of the user set as the host of the archived meeting or webinar.

type.description:
The type of archived meeting or webinar. 

 If the recording is of a meeting: 
* 1 - Instant meeting. 
* 2 - Scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A meeting created via PMI (Personal Meeting ID). 
* 7 - A [Personal Audio Conference](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204517069-Getting-Started-with-Personal-Audio-Conference) (PAC). 
* 8 - Recurring meeting with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is of a webinar: 
* 5 - A webinar. 
* 6 - A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 - A recurring webinar with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is **not** from a meeting or webinar: 

* 100 - A [breakout room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115005769646-Participating-in-breakout-rooms).

start_time.description: The meeting or webinar's start time.

duration.description: The meeting or webinar's scheduled duration.

duration_in_second.description: The meeting or webinar's duration, in seconds.

total_size.description: The total size of the archive file, in bytes.

recording_count.description: The number of archived files returned in the API call response.

meeting_type.description:
Whether the meeting or webinar is internal or external. 
* internal - An internal meeting or webinar. 
* external - An external meeting or webinar. 

 The id, host_id, and topic PII (Personal Identifiable Information) values in this response are removed when this value is external.

complete_time.description: The meeting or webinar's archive completion time.

parent_meeting_id.description: The parent meeting's universally unique ID (UUID). Each meeting or webinar instance generates its own meeting UUID. If the is_breakout_room value is true, the API returns this value.

file_type.description:
The archived file's type. 
* MP4 - Video file. 
* M4A - Audio-only file. 
* TRANSCRIPT - A transcription file, in VTT format. 
* CHAT - A TXT file containing in-meeting chat messages. 
* CC - A file containing the closed captions of the recording, in VTT file format. 
* CHAT_MESSAGE - A JSON file encoded in base64 format containing chat messages. The file also includes waiting room chats, deleted messages, meeting emojis and non-verbal feedback.

file_size.description: The archived file's size, in bytes.

download_url.description:
The URL to download the archived file. To download the archived file of a meeting or a webinar, use the download_token value included in the webhook as the access_token query parameter value and include this query parameter at the end of the URL. For example: 

 https://{{base-domain}}/rec/archive/download/xxx?access_token={{your-download_token-value}}

status.description:
The archived file's processing status. 
* completed - The processing of the file is complete. 
* processing - The file is processing. 
* failed - The file processing failed.

recording_type.description:
The archive file's recording type. 
* shared_screen_with_speaker_view 
* audio_only 
* chat_file 
* closed_caption 
* chat_message 

 For more information, read our [Managing and sharing cloud recordings](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205347605-Managing-and-sharing-cloud-recordings#h_9898497b-e736-4980-a749-d55608f10773) documentation.

individual.description:
Whether the recording file is an individual recording file: 
* true - An individual recording file. 
 * false - An entire meeting file.

participant_email.description: The individual recording file's participant email address. This value is returned when the individual value is true. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, this returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

participant_join_time.description: The join time for the generated recording file. If this value is returned when the individual value is true, then it is the recording file's participant join time. When the individual value is false, it returns the join time for the archiving gateway.

participant_leave_time.description: The leave time for the generated recording file. If this value is returned when the individual value is true, then it is the recording file's participant leave time. When the individual value is false, it returns the leave time for the archiving gateway.

encryption_fingerprint.description: The archived file's encryption fingerprint, using the SHA256 hash algorithm.

number_of_messages.description: The number of TXT or JSON file messages. This field will return only when the file_extension is JSON or TXT

storage_location.description: The region where the file is stored. This field returns only Enable Distributed Compliance Archiving op feature is enabled.

status.description:
The archive's processing status: 
* completed - The archive's processing is complete. 
* processing - The archive is processing.

group_id.description: Primary group IDs of participants who belong to your account. Each group ID is separated by a comma.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

download_token.description:
Use the generated token value with the download_url value to download the cloud recording via an [OAuth app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/getting-started/app-types/create-oauth-app). The download token only lasts for 24 hours after its creation, and you can only download the file within 24 hours of receiving the event notification. You can either include the download_token as a query parameter or pass it as a Bearer token in the Authorization header of your HTTP request. 

 **Using an Authorization header (Recommended)** 

 
curl --request GET \
  --url {download_url} \
  --header 'authorization: Bearer {download_token} \
  --header 'content-type: application/json'
 

**Using a query parameter** 

 
{download_url}/?access_token={download_token}
 

 For example: https://zoom.us/recording/download/123456?access_token=abcdefgh

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who hosted the meeting or the webinar.

object.description: Information about the webinar or meeting recording.

uuid.description: The universally unique identifier (UUID) of the recorded meeting or webinar instance. Each meeting or webinar instance generates its own meeting UUID.

id.description: The ID of the meeting (meetingId) or the webinar (webinarId).

host_id.description: The ID of the user set as the host of the archived meeting or webinar.

type.description:
The type of archived meeting or webinar. 

 If the recording is of a meeting: 
* 1 - Instant meeting. 
* 2 - Scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A meeting created via PMI (Personal Meeting ID). 
* 7 - A [Personal Audio Conference](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204517069-Getting-Started-with-Personal-Audio-Conference) (PAC). 
* 8 - Recurring meeting with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is of a webinar: 
* 5 - A webinar. 
* 6 - A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 - A recurring webinar with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is **not** from a meeting or webinar: 

* 100 - A [breakout room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115005769646-Participating-in-breakout-rooms).

duration.description: The meeting or webinar's scheduled duration.

duration_in_second.description: The meeting or webinar's duration, in seconds.

* internal - An internal meeting or webinar. 
* external - An external meeting or webinar. 

 The id, host_id, and topic PII (Personal Identifiable Information) values in this response are removed when this value is external.

complete_time.description: The meeting or webinar's archive completion time.

parent_meeting_id.description: The parent meeting's universally unique ID (UUID). Each meeting or webinar instance generates its own meeting UUID. If the is_breakout_room value is true, the API returns this value.

file_type.description:
The archived file's type. 
* MP4 - Video file. 
* M4A - Audio-only file. 
* TRANSCRIPT - A transcription file, in VTT format. 
* CHAT - A TXT file containing in-meeting chat messages. 
* CC - A file containing the closed captions of the recording, in VTT file format. 
* CHAT_MESSAGE - A JSON file encoded in base64 format containing chat messages. The file also includes waiting room chats, deleted messages, meeting emojis and non-verbal feedback.

If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, this returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

# meeting.alert

The **Meeting issues alert** event is triggered every time a service issue is encountered during a meeting scheduled by a user in your account or in accounts that have installed your [webhook-enabled](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#enable-webhooks) app.

Zoom monitors [meeting statistics](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/202920719-Meeting-Statistics) in regards to the quality of service for each meeting. The following quality metrics can trigger an alert:

* Unstable audio.
* Unstable video.
* Poor screen share quality.
* High CPU usage.
* Call reconnection problems.

## Prerequisites

* The account **must** hold a [Business or a higher plan](https://zoom.us/pricing) to request this feature.
* In account settings, on the dashboard, enable the **Meeting quality scores and network alerts on Dashboard** option.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Meeting issues alert** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:alert:master`,`meeting:read:alert`,`meeting:read:alert:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.alert`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

issues.description:
A list of issues that occurred during the meeting: 
* Unstable audio quality 
* Unstable video quality 
* Unstable screen share quality 
* High CPU occupation 
* Call Reconnection

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

# meeting.chat_message_file_sent

The **In-meeting chat message file received** event is triggered when a meeting's message file is available to view or download. The event is sent to the configured endpoint in your Marketplace app.

## Prerequisites

* Enable the **Enable in-meeting chat DLP (Data Loss Prevention) integration** and **Include in-meeting chat files** features in the host's account.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **In-meeting chat message file received** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.
* This feature is **only** enabled upon request approval. Contact the [Zoom support team](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/contact?id=contact_us) for more information.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:chat_message:master`,`meeting:read:chat_message`,`meeting:read:chat_message:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.chat_message_file_sent`

event_ts.description: The timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who hosted the meeting.

object.description: Information about the chat message file.

meeting_id.description: The meeting ID.

meeting_uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates its own meeting UUID.

breakout_room_uuid.description: The breakout room's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each breakout room instance generates a breakout room UUID.

chat_message_file.description: Information about the chat message file.

date_time.description: The date and time when the chat message was sent.

sender_type.description:
The type of user that sent the message. 
* host - The meeting host. 
* guest - A meeting guest.

sender_context.description:
The context where the sender is located when the message is generated. 
* meeting - The sender is in a meeting. 
* breakout_room - The sender is in a breakout room.

recipient_email.description: The message recipient's email address. Optional only included if it is a private message.

recipient_type.description:
The type of message recipient. 
* everyone - All meeting participants. 
* host - The meeting host. 
* guest - A meeting guest. 
* group - A group chat. 
* host_group - The meeting host and all co-hosts.

recipient_context.description:
The context where the recipient is located when the message is delivered. 
* meeting - The recipient is in the meeting. 
* breakout_room - The sender is in a breakout room.

file_id.description: The live meeting chat file's UUID, in base64-encoded format.

file_name.description: The live meeting chat file's name.

file_size.description: The live meeting chat file's size, in bytes.

file_type.description: The live meeting chat file's type.

download_url.description: The live meeting chat file's download URL.

# meeting.deleted

The **Meeting has been deleted** event is triggered every time a meeting scheduled by one of your app users or account users, is deleted.

A meeting host or a user with admin privileges can delete a scheduled meeting. Users can delete a meeting manually using the **Delete** feature for a meeting via the [Zoom web portal](https://zoom.us) or the Zoom client. Users can also programmatically delete meetings through the [**Delete a Meeting**](/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/#operation/meetingDelete) API.

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or higher plan.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Meeting has been deleted** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:meeting:master`,`meeting:read:meeting`,`meeting:read:meeting:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.deleted`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who deleted the meeting.

operator.description: The email address of the user who deleted the meeting.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who deleted the meeting.

operation.description:
The type of operation performed: 
* all — The change was applied to all meetings. 
* single — The change was applied only to a single meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

occurrences.description: Information about recurring meetings with fixed times and their occurrences. This only returns when the meeting's type value is 8.

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who deleted the meeting.

operator.description: The email address of the user who deleted the meeting.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who deleted the meeting.

operation.description:
The type of operation performed: 
* all — The change was applied to all meetings. 
* single — The change was applied only to a single meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

occurrences.description: Information about recurring meetings with fixed times and their occurrences. This only returns when the meeting's type value is 8.

# meeting.participant_joined

The **Participant or Host joined meeting** event is triggered every time an attendee joins a meeting. A meeting attendee is a meeting participant or the host. The meeting host must be a user in your account or a user in any other accounts that have installed your [webhook-enabled](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/webhook-reference/#enable-webhooks) app.

## Prerequisites

* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Participant or Host joined meeting** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant:master`,`meeting:read:participant`,`meeting:read:participant:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.participant_joined`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

participant.description: Information about the meeting participant. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, email returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

user_id.description: The participant's meeting user ID. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value. 

 This value returns blank for external users.

**Note:** Use the participant_user_id value instead of this value. We will remove this response in a future release.

participant_uuid.description: The participant's UUID for this specific meeting and any breakout rooms created in this meeting. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of that meeting.

join_time.description: The time when the participant joined the meeting.

email.description: The participant's email address. This response only returns if the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, this returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

registrant_id.description: The participant's registrant ID. A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026).

participant_user_id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

 This value returns blank for external users.

customer_key.description: The participant's SDK identifier. This value can be alphanumeric.

phone_number.description: Phone number of participant joined via PSTN.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.
