See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

user_id.description: The participant's meeting user ID. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value. 

 This value returns blank for external users.

**Note:** Use the participant_user_id value instead of this value. We will remove this response in a future release.

participant_uuid.description: The participant's UUID for this specific meeting and any breakout rooms created in this meeting. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of that meeting.

join_time.description: The time when the participant joined the meeting.

email.description: The participant's email address. This response only returns if the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, this returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

registrant_id.description: The participant's registrant ID. A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026).

participant_user_id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

 This value returns blank for external users.

customer_key.description: The participant's SDK identifier. This value can be alphanumeric.

phone_number.description: Phone number of participant joined via PSTN.

# user.tsp_deleted

The **A user's TSP account was deleted** event is triggered every time a TSP user account is deleted.

Zoom allows certain carrier partners to provision Telephony Service Provider (TSP) audio to users. For users to use TSP audio, carrier partners must create a TSP user account with Zoom for the users. Zoom account owners and admins can [delete an existing TSP user account](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/userTSPDelete).

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or higher plan with TSP Configurations enabled.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* The **A user's TSP account was deleted** subscription must be enabled under the **TSP** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `tsp:read:admin`,`tsp:write:admin`,`tsp:read`,`tsp:write`,`tsp:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `tsp:read:tsp_account:master`,`tsp:read:tsp_account`,`tsp:read:tsp_account:admin`

**Event type**: `user.tsp_deleted`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who deleted the TSP.

operator.description: The email address of the user who deleted the TSP.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who deleted the TSP.

tsp_credentials.description: Information about the deleted user's TSP credentials.

dial_in_numbers.description: Information about the TSP dial-in numbers.

type.description:
The type of the phone number: 
* toll — Toll number. 
* tollfree — Toll free number. 
* media_link — Media link.

country_label.description:
The TSP country label: 
* US_TSP_TB 
* EU_TSP_TB

* tollfree — Toll free number.

# meeting.ai_companion_started
The **AI Companion started** event is triggered when a user starts the AI Companion during a live meeting.

## Prerequisites

* Host user must be Pro or higher plan.
* Enable [AI Companion](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0057623).
* E2EE meetings do not support the AI Companion feature.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **AI Companion started** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read`,`meeting:read:admin`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:ai_companion`,`meeting:read:ai_companion:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.ai_companion_started`

event_ts.description: The timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user that hosted the meeting.

object.description: The meeting where the AI Companion was started.

meeting_uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID).

host_id.description: The ID of the user set as the host of the meeting.

ai_companion.description: The AI Companion features that were started.

questions.description: Whether the AI Companion's meeting questions feature was started.

summary.description: Whether the AI Companion's meeting summary feature was started.

event_ts.description: The timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user that hosted the meeting.

object.description: The meeting where the AI Companion was started.

meeting_uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID).

host_id.description: The ID of the user set as the host of the meeting.

ai_companion.description: The AI Companion features that were started.

questions.description: Whether the AI Companion's meeting questions feature was started.

summary.description: Whether the AI Companion's meeting summary feature was started.

# meeting.invitation_dispatched

The **Invited party's Zoom is ringing** event is triggered every time a user sends an invitation to join a meeting or video call to one or more recipients.

This event is only suitable for triggering with the ZOOM client.

## Prerequisites

* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with these configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Invited party's Zoom is ringing** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.





**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:invitation`,`meeting:read:invitation:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.invitation_dispatched`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The meeting host's account ID.

operator.description: The email address of the user who sent the meeting invitation.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who sent the meeting invitation.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

participant_user_id.description: The user ID of the user who received the meeting invitation.

email.description: The email address of the user who received the meeting invitation. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, the email field returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. For more information, read our [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) documentation.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The meeting host's account ID.

operator.description: The email address of the user who sent the meeting invitation.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who sent the meeting invitation.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

participant_user_id.description: The user ID of the user who received the meeting invitation.

email.description: The email address of the user who received the meeting invitation. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, the email field returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. For more information, read our [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) documentation.

# webinar.ended

The **End Webinar** event is triggered every time a user in your account or your app ends a webinar.

Zoom users with a [Webinar Plan](https://zoom.us/webinar) have permissions for creating and managing webinars. [Zoom Video Webinar](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/200917029-Getting-Started-With-Webinar) allows users to broadcast a Zoom meeting to up to 1000 attendees. A webinar host can start and end a webinar.

## Prerequisites

* Pro or a higher plan with Webinar license.
* **Event Subscription** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **End Webinar** subscription enabled under the **Webinar** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:admin`,`webinar:write:admin`,`webinar:read`,`webinar:write`,`webinar:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:webinar:master`,`webinar:read:webinar`,`webinar:read:webinar:admin`

**Event type**: `webinar.ended`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who ended the webinar.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

type.description:
The webinar type. 
* 5 - A webinar. 
* 6 - A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 - A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

end_time.description: The date and time when the webinar ended.

practice_session.description: whether this webinar is a practice session.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who ended the webinar.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

type.description:
The webinar type. 
* 5 - A webinar. 
* 6 - A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 - A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

end_time.description: The date and time when the webinar ended.

practice_session.description: whether this webinar is a practice session.

# meeting.converted_to_webinar
The **Meeting has been converted to a Webinar** event is triggered every time one of your app users or account users converts a meeting into a webinar.

## Prerequisites

* Pro or a higher plan with Webinar license.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Meeting has been converted to a Webinar** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

* 5 - A scheduled webinar.
* 6 - A recurring webinar with no fixed time.

start_time.description: The scheduled webinar's start time. Returned only when the webinar type is 5, a scheduled webinar.

duration.description: The scheduled webinar's duration, in minutes. Returned only when the webinar type is 5, ascheduled webinar.

# webinar.recovered

The **Webinar has been recovered** event is triggered every time a user recovers a previously-deleted webinar. The user must be a user on your account or on any other accounts that have installed your [webhook-enabled](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#enable-webhooks) app.

Zoom users can restore previously-deleted webinars for up to one week after deletion. Any invitations, links, and webinar IDs sent prior to the webinar's deletion will still work after a user [recovers](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360038297111-Recovering-a-deleted-meeting-or-webinar) it.

## Prerequisites

* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Webinar has been recovered** subscription enabled under the **Webinar** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:admin`,`webinar:write:admin`,`webinar:read`,`webinar:write`,`webinar:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:webinar:master`,`webinar:read:webinar`,`webinar:read:webinar:admin`

**Event type**: `webinar.recovered`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

The type of operation performed:

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

# webinar.participant_role_changed

The **Participant role changed** event is triggered every time a host or webinar attendee changes the role during a webinar created by one of your app users or account users. A webinar host can invite other users to attend the webinar.

Zoom users with a [Webinar plan](https://zoom.us/webinar) have access to creating and managing webinars. [Zoom webinars](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/200917029-Getting-Started-With-Webinar) let users to broadcast a Zoom meeting to up to 1,000 attendees.

## Prerequisites

* Pro or a higher plan with Webinar license.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Participant role changed** subscription enabled under the **Webinar** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:admin`,`webinar:write:admin`,`webinar:read`,`webinar:write`,`webinar:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:participant:master`,`webinar:read:participant`,`webinar:read:participant:admin`

**Event type**: `webinar.participant_role_changed`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the webinar.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

type.description:
The webinar type: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

user_id.description: The participant's webinar user ID. This value is assigned to a participant upon joining a webinar and is only valid for the duration of the webinar.

email.description: The participant's email address. This response only returns if the participant joined the webinar by logging into Zoom. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, this returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. For more information, read our [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) documentation.

registrant_id.description: The participant's registrant ID. (A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026)).

participant_user_id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the webinar by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the webinar **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

This value returns blank for external users

participant_uuid.description: The participant's UUID for this specific webinar. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a webinar, and is only valid for the duration of that webinar.

date_time.description: The time when the participant changed the [role](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360000252726-Roles-in-a-Zoom-Webinar).

old_role.description:
The participant's previous role. 
* host — Host. 
* co-host — Co-host. 
* attendee — Attendee.

new_role.description:
The participant's new role. 
* host — Host. 
* co-host — Co-host. 
* attendee — Attendee.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the webinar.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

type.description:
The webinar type: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

user_id.description: The participant's webinar user ID. This value is assigned to a participant upon joining a webinar and is only valid for the duration of the webinar.

email.description: The participant's email address. This response only returns if the participant joined the webinar by logging into Zoom. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, this returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. For more information, read our [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) documentation.

participant_user_id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the webinar by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the webinar **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

This value returns blank for external users

participant_uuid.description: The participant's UUID for this specific webinar. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a webinar, and is only valid for the duration of that webinar.

date_time.description: The time when the participant changed the [role](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360000252726-Roles-in-a-Zoom-Webinar).

old_role.description:
The participant's previous role. 
* host — Host. 
* co-host — Co-host. 
* attendee — Attendee.

new_role.description:
The participant's new role. 
* host — Host. 
* co-host — Co-host. 
* attendee — Attendee.

# meeting.participant_jbh_waiting_left

The **Waiting participant left meeting before host joined** event is triggered every time an attendee waiting for the host to join the meeting leaves before the host. The host of the meeting must be a user in your account or users in any other accounts that have installed your [webhook-enabled](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/webhook-reference/#enable-webhooks) app.

When scheduling a meeting, the organizer or host of the meeting can enable the **Enable join before host** option. This allows attendees to join the meeting before the host of the meeting arrives. This meeting also starts as soon as the first meeting participant joins.

If this option is **disabled**, participants are prompted to wait for the host to arrive before joining the meeting.

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or a higher plan.
* Disable the **Enable join before host** option for the scheduled meeting.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Waiting participant left meeting before host joined** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.






**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant:master`,`meeting:read:participant`,`meeting:read:participant:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.participant_jbh_waiting_left`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (personal audio conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

customer_key.description: The participant's SDK identifier. This value can be alphanumeric.

registrant_id.description: The participant's registrant ID. A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026).

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (personal audio conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

customer_key.description: The participant's SDK identifier. This value can be alphanumeric.

registrant_id.description: The participant's registrant ID. A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026).

# user.tsp_created

The **A user's TSP account was created** event is triggered every time a [TSP user account is created](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/userTSPCreate) in Zoom.

Zoom allows certain carrier partners to provision Telephony Service Provider (TSP) audio to users. For users to use TSP audio, carrier partners must create a TSP user account with Zoom for the users.

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or higher plan with TSP Configurations enabled.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* The **A user's TSP account was created** subscription must be enabled under the **TSP** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `tsp:read:admin`,`tsp:write:admin`,`tsp:read`,`tsp:write`,`tsp:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `tsp:read:tsp_account:master`,`tsp:read:tsp_account`,`tsp:read:tsp_account:admin`

**Event type**: `user.tsp_created`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the TSP.

operator.description: The email address of the user who created the TSP.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who created the TSP.

tsp_credentials.description: Information about the user's TSP credentials.

dial_in_numbers.description: Information about the TSP dial-in numbers.

type.description:
The type of the phone number: 
* toll — Toll number. 
* tollfree — Toll free number. 
* media_link — Media link.

country_label.description:
The TSP country label: 
* US_TSP_TB 
* EU_TSP_TB

The TSP country label:

# meeting.breakout_room_sharing_ended

The **User in breakout room stopped sharing** event is triggered when a user in a [breakout room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115005769646-Participating-in-breakout-rooms) ends their [content-sharing](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362153-Sharing-your-screen-or-desktop-on-Zoom), such as their desktop or the [classic whiteboard](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205677665-Sharing-a-whiteboard).

Breakout rooms are sessions that are split off from the main Zoom meeting. They allow the participants to meet in smaller groups and are completely isolated in terms of audio and video from the main session.

## Prerequisites

* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **User in breakout room stopped sharing** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant_sharing:master`,`meeting:read:participant_sharing`,`meeting:read:participant_sharing:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.breakout_room_sharing_ended`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The main meeting host's account ID.

uuid.description: The main meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

breakout_room_uuid.description: The breakout room's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each breakout room instance generates a breakout room UUID.

host_id.description: The main meeting host's user ID.

type.description:
The main meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

user_id.description: The participant's meeting user ID. This value is assigned to a participant upon joining a meeting and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

parent_user_id.description: The participant's main meeting user ID (user_id).

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID): 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

content.description:
The type of shared content: 
* application — An application. For example, a web browser. 
* whiteboard — The [classic whiteboard](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205677665-Sharing-a-whiteboard). 
* desktop — A user's desktop. 
* airplay — A user's airplay. 
* camera — A user's camera. 
* unknown — An unrecognized application, such as a third party app.

link_source.description:
The Dropbox sharing method: 
* deep_link — A [deep link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_linking). 
* in_meeting — A link shared via Zoom. 
* An empty string value if the link source is **not** a Dropbox link.

file_link.description: The Dropbox file's link. This field returns an empty string value if the file link is **not** from a Dropbox source.

date_time.description: The screenshare's date and time.

source.description:
The source from which the file was shared: 
* dropbox — A Dropbox source. 
* An empty string value if the source is **not** Dropbox.

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The main meeting host's account ID.

uuid.description: The main meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

breakout_room_uuid.description: The breakout room's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each breakout room instance generates a breakout room UUID.

host_id.description: The main meeting host's user ID.

type.description:
The main meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

user_id.description: The participant's meeting user ID. This value is assigned to a participant upon joining a meeting and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

parent_user_id.description: The participant's main meeting user ID (user_id).

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID): 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

content.description:
The type of shared content: 
* application — An application. For example, a web browser. 
* whiteboard — The [classic whiteboard](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205677665-Sharing-a-whiteboard). 
* desktop — A user's desktop. 
* airplay — A user's airplay. 
* camera — A user's camera. 
* unknown — An unrecognized application, such as a third party app.

link_source.description:
The Dropbox sharing method: 
* deep_link — A [deep link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_linking). 
* in_meeting — A link shared via Zoom. 
* An empty string value if the link source is **not** a Dropbox link.

file_link.description: The Dropbox file's link. This field returns an empty string value if the file link is **not** from a Dropbox source.

date_time.description: The screenshare's date and time.

source.description:
The source from which the file was shared: 
* dropbox — A Dropbox source. 
* An empty string value if the source is **not** Dropbox.

# meeting.updated

The **Meeting has been updated** event is triggered when a meeting scheduled by one of your app users or account users is updated.

Meeting hosts or users with admin privileges can update scheduled meetings.

You can update the meeting manually through the **Edit** feature in the [Zoom web portal](https://zoom.us) or the Zoom client, or programmatically update a meeting through the [**Update a meeting**](/docs/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/meetingUpdate) API.

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or higher plan.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**
	* The **Meeting has been updated** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:meeting:master`,`meeting:read:meeting`,`meeting:read:meeting:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.updated`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who updated the meeting.

operator.description: The email address of the user who updated the meeting.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who updated the meeting.

scope.description:
The type of operation performed. 
* all - The change was applied to all meetings. 
* single - The change was applied only to a single meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time. 
* 10 - A screen share only meeting.

join_url.description: The URL participants use to join the meeting.

password.description: The password required to join the meeting.

occurrences.description: Information about recurring meetings with fixed times and their occurrences. This only returns when the meeting's type value is 8.

status.description:
The meeting occurrence status. 
* available 
* deleted

settings.description: Information about the updated meeting settings. This **only** returns updated meeting settings.

host_video.description: Whether to start the meeting with the host's video on.

participant_video.description: Whether to turn a participant's video on when they join the meeting. Participants can change this setting during the meeting.

join_before_host.description: Whether to [allow participants to join the meeting before host arrives](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/202828525-Allow-participants-to-join-before-host).

jbh_time.description:
If the value of the join_before_host field is true, this field indicates the time limits when a participant can join a meeting before the meeting's host.

* 0 - Allow the participant to join the meeting at any time.
* 5 - Allow the participant to join 5 minutes before the meeting's start time.
* 10 - Allow the participant to join 10 minutes before the meeting's start time.

mute_upon_entry.description: Whether participants are muted upon entry.

audio.description:
The available audio options for participants joining the meeting. 
* telephony - Telephony only. 
* voip - VoIP only. 
* both - Both Telephony and VoIP.

auto_recording.description:
The [automatic recording](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/202921119-Automatic-Recording) settings for the meeting. 
* local - Record and save to the local device. 
* cloud - Record and save to the cloud. 
* none - Automatic recording disabled.

use_pmi.description: Whether to use a [Personal Meeting ID (PMI)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203276937-Using-Personal-Meeting-ID-PMI) instead of an auto-generated meeting ID. PMIs are only available for scheduled meetings, instant meetings, and recurring meetings with no fixed time.

enforce_login.description: Whether to only allow signed in users to join the meeting.

enforce_login_domains.description: A list of the domains allowed to join the meeting.

approval_type.description:
The [approval type](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/211579443-Scheduling-a-meeting-with-registration) for meeting registrants. 
* 0 - Automatically approve registrants. 
* 1 - Manually approve registrants. 
* 2 - Meeting registration not required.

registration_type.description:
The meeting registration setting for recurring meetings. 
* 1 - Attendees register once and can attend any of the meeting occurrences. 
* 2 - Attendees must register for each occurrence to attend. 
* 3 - Attendees register once and can select one or more occurrences to attend.

alternative_hosts.description: A semicolon-separated list of the meeting's alternative hosts' email addresses or IDs.

meeting_authentication.description: Whether only authenticated users can join the meeting.

authentication_name.description: The authentication name set in the [authentication profile](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360037117472).

authentication_domains.description: A list of the allowed authenticated domains.

meeting_invitees.description: A list of the meeting's invitees.

enable.description: Whether to enable [language interpretation](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064768) for the meeting.

interpreters.description: Information about the meeting's language interpreters.

languages.description:
A comma-separated list of the interpreter's languages. The string must contain exactly two country IDs.

Only system-supported languages are allowed: US (English), CN (Chinese), JP (Japanese), DE (German), FR (French), RU (Russian), PT (Portuguese), ES (Spanish), and KR (Korean).

For example, to set an interpreter translating from English to Chinese, use US,CN.

interpreter_languages.description:
A comma-separated list of the interpreter's languages. The string must contain exactly two languages.

To get this value, use the language_interpretation object's languages and custom_languages values in the [**Get user settings**](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/users/#tag/users/GET/users/{userId}/settings) API response.

**languages**: System-supported languages include English, Chinese, Japanese, German, French, Russian, Portuguese, Spanish, and Korean.

**custom_languages**: User-defined languages added by the user.

For example, an interpreter translating between English and French should use English,French.

language_interpretation.description:
The meeting's [language interpretation settings](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360034919791-Language-interpretation-in-meetings-and-webinars). Make sure to add the language in the web portal in order to use it in the API. See link for details. 

**Note:** This feature is only available for certain Meeting add-on, Education, and Business and higher plans. If this feature is not enabled on the host's account, this setting will **not** be applied to the meeting.

enable.description: Whether to enable [sign language interpretation](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/9644962487309-Using-sign-language-interpretation-in-a-meeting-or-webinar) for the meeting.

interpreters.description: Information about the meeting's sign language interpreters.

sign_language.description:
The interpreter's sign language. 

 To get this value, use the sign_language_interpretation object's languages and custom_languages values in the [**Get user settings**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/userSettings) API response.

sign_language_interpretation.description:
The meeting's [sign language interpretation settings](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/9644962487309-Using-sign-language-interpretation-in-a-meeting-or-webinar). Make sure to add the language in the web portal in order to use it in the API. See link for details. 

**Note:** If this feature is not enabled on the host's account, this setting will **not** be applied to the meeting.

enable.description: Whether to enable the **Enable continuous meeting chat** setting.

auto_add_invited_external_users.description: Whether to enable the **Automatically add invited external users** setting.

auto_add_meeting_participants.description: Whether to enable the **Automatically add meeting participants** setting.

continuous_meeting_chat.description: Information about the **Enable continuous meeting chat** feature.

auto_start_meeting_summary.description: Whether to automatically start a meeting summary.

who_will_receive_summary.description:
Defines who will receive a summary after this meeting. This field is applicable only when auto_start_meeting_summary is set to true.
* 1 - Only meeting host.
* 2 - Only meeting host, co-hosts, and alternative hosts.
* 3 - Only meeting host and meeting invitees in our organization.
* 4 - All meeting invitees including those outside of our organization.

auto_start_ai_companion_questions.description: Whether to automatically start AI Companion questions.

who_can_ask_questions.description:
Defines who can ask questions about this meeting's transcript. This field is applicable only when auto_start_ai_companion_questions is set to true.
* 1 - All participants and invitees.
* 2 - All participants only from when they join.
* 3 - Only meeting host.
* 4 - Participants and invitees in our organization.
* 5 - Participants in our organization only from when they join.

summary_template_id.description: The summary template ID used to generate a meeting summary based on a predefined template. To get available summary templates, use the **Get user summary templates** API. To enable this feature for your account, please [contact Zoom Support](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en).

allow_host_control_participant_mute_stat.description: Whether to allow host and co-hosts to fully control the mute state of participants.

email_in_attendee_report.description: Whether to include authenticated guest's email addresses in meetings' attendee reports.

type.description:
The type of recurring meeting. 
* 1 - Daily. 
* 2 - Weekly. 
* 3 - Monthly.

repeat_interval.description:
The meeting's repeat interval. 
* For daily intervals, a max of 99 days. 
* For a weekly interval, a max of 50 weeks. 
* For monthly intervals, a max of 10 months.

weekly_days.description:
A comma-separated list of the days of the week when the recurring weekly meeting occurs. 
* 1 - Sunday 
* 2 - Monday 
* 3 - Tuesday 
* 4 - Wednesday 
* 5 - Thursday 
* 6 - Friday 
* 7 - Saturday

monthly_day.description: The day of the month when the recurring monthly meeting occurs. The value ranges from 1 to 31.

monthly_week_day.description:
The day of the week when the recurring monthly meeting is scheduled. 
* 1 - Sunday 
* 2 - Monday 
* 3 - Tuesday 
* 4 - Wednesday 
* 5 - Thursday 
* 6 - Friday 
* 7 - Saturday

end_times.description: The number of times the recurring meeting will occur before it is canceled.

end_date_time.description: The recurring meeting's final ending date and time before it is canceled, in UTC format.

monthly_week.description:
The week of the month when a recurring meeting occurs. 
* -1 - The last week of the month. 
* 1 - The first week. 
* 2 - The second week. 
* 3 - The third week. 
* 4 - The fourth week.

time_stamp.description: The meeting's update timestamp, in milliseconds.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who updated the meeting.

operator.description: The email address of the user who updated the meeting.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who updated the meeting.

scope.description:
The type of operation performed. 
* all - The change was applied to all meetings. 
* single - The change was applied only to a single meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time. 
* 10 - A screen share only meeting.

join_url.description: The URL participants use to join the meeting.

password.description: The password required to join the meeting.

occurrences.description: Information about recurring meetings with fixed times and their occurrences. This only returns when the meeting's type value is 8.

status.description:
The meeting occurrence status. 
* available 
* deleted

settings.description: Information about the updated meeting settings. This **only** returns updated meeting settings.

host_video.description: Whether to start the meeting with the host's video on.

participant_video.description: Whether to turn a participant's video on when they join the meeting. Participants can change this setting during the meeting.

join_before_host.description: Whether to [allow participants to join the meeting before host arrives](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/202828525-Allow-participants-to-join-before-host).

jbh_time.description:
If the value of the join_before_host field is true, this field indicates the time limits when a participant can join a meeting before the meeting's host.

* 0 - Allow the participant to join the meeting at any time.
* 5 - Allow the participant to join 5 minutes before the meeting's start time.
* 10 - Allow the participant to join 10 minutes before the meeting's start time.

mute_upon_entry.description: Whether participants are muted upon entry.

audio.description:
The available audio options for participants joining the meeting. 
* telephony - Telephony only. 
* voip - VoIP only. 
* both - Both Telephony and VoIP.

auto_recording.description:
The [automatic recording](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/202921119-Automatic-Recording) settings for the meeting. 
* local - Record and save to the local device. 
* cloud - Record and save to the cloud. 
* none - Automatic recording disabled.

use_pmi.description: Whether to use a [Personal Meeting ID (PMI)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203276937-Using-Personal-Meeting-ID-PMI) instead of an auto-generated meeting ID. PMIs are only available for scheduled meetings, instant meetings, and recurring meetings with no fixed time.

enforce_login.description: Whether to only allow signed in users to join the meeting.

enforce_login_domains.description: A list of the domains allowed to join the meeting.

approval_type.description:
The [approval type](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/211579443-Scheduling-a-meeting-with-registration) for meeting registrants. 
* 0 - Automatically approve registrants. 
* 1 - Manually approve registrants. 
* 2 - Meeting registration not required.

registration_type.description:
The meeting registration setting for recurring meetings. 
* 1 - Attendees register once and can attend any of the meeting occurrences. 
* 2 - Attendees must register for each occurrence to attend. 
* 3 - Attendees register once and can select one or more occurrences to attend.

alternative_hosts.description: A semicolon-separated list of the meeting's alternative hosts' email addresses or IDs.

meeting_authentication.description: Whether only authenticated users can join the meeting.

authentication_name.description: The authentication name set in the [authentication profile](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360037117472).

authentication_domains.description: A list of the allowed authenticated domains.

meeting_invitees.description: A list of the meeting's invitees.

enable.description: Whether to enable [language interpretation](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064768) for the meeting.

interpreters.description: Information about the meeting's language interpreters.

languages.description:
A comma-separated list of the interpreter's languages. The string must contain exactly two country IDs.

Only system-supported languages are allowed: US (English), CN (Chinese), JP (Japanese), DE (German), FR (French), RU (Russian), PT (Portuguese), ES (Spanish), and KR (Korean).

For example, to set an interpreter translating from English to Chinese, use US,CN.

interpreter_languages.description:
A comma-separated list of the interpreter's languages. The string must contain exactly two languages.

To get this value, use the language_interpretation object's languages and custom_languages values in the [**Get user settings**](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/users/#tag/users/GET/users/{userId}/settings) API response.

**languages**: System-supported languages include English, Chinese, Japanese, German, French, Russian, Portuguese, Spanish, and Korean.

**custom_languages**: User-defined languages added by the user.

For example, an interpreter translating between English and French should use English,French.

language_interpretation.description:
The meeting's [language interpretation settings](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360034919791-Language-interpretation-in-meetings-and-webinars). Make sure to add the language in the web portal in order to use it in the API. See link for details. 

**Note:** This feature is only available for certain Meeting add-on, Education, and Business and higher plans. If this feature is not enabled on the host's account, this setting will **not** be applied to the meeting.

enable.description: Whether to enable [sign language interpretation](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/9644962487309-Using-sign-language-interpretation-in-a-meeting-or-webinar) for the meeting.

interpreters.description: Information about the meeting's sign language interpreters.

sign_language.description:
The interpreter's sign language. 

 To get this value, use the sign_language_interpretation object's languages and custom_languages values in the [**Get user settings**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/userSettings) API response.

sign_language_interpretation.description:
The meeting's [sign language interpretation settings](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/9644962487309-Using-sign-language-interpretation-in-a-meeting-or-webinar). Make sure to add the language in the web portal in order to use it in the API. See link for details. 

**Note:** If this feature is not enabled on the host's account, this setting will **not** be applied to the meeting.

auto_add_invited_external_users.description: Whether to enable the **Automatically add invited external users** setting.

auto_add_meeting_participants.description: Whether to enable the **Automatically add meeting participants** setting.

continuous_meeting_chat.description: Information about the **Enable continuous meeting chat** feature.

* 1 - Only meeting host.
* 2 - Only meeting host, co-hosts, and alternative hosts.
* 3 - Only meeting host and meeting invitees in our organization.

* 3 - Only meeting host.

* 5 - Participants in our organization only from when they join.

The type of recurring meeting.

repeat_interval.description:
The meeting's repeat interval. 
* For daily intervals, a max of 99 days. 
* For a weekly interval, a max of 50 weeks.

monthly_day.description: The day of the month when the recurring monthly meeting occurs. The value ranges from 1 to 31.

# meeting.ai_companion_stopped
The **AI Companion stopped** event is triggered when a user stops the AI Companion during a live meeting.

## Prerequisites

* Host user must be on a Pro or higher plan.
* Enable [AI Companion](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0057623).
* E2EE meetings do not support the AI Companion feature.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **AI Companion stopped** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read`,`meeting:read:admin`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:ai_companion`,`meeting:read:ai_companion:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.ai_companion_stopped`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user that hosted the meeting.

object.description: The meeting where the AI Companion was stopped.

meeting_uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID).

host_id.description: The ID of the user set as the host of the meeting.

ai_companion.description: The AI Companion features that were stopped.

questions.description: Whether the AI Companion's meeting questions feature was stopped.

summary.description: Whether the AI Companion's meeting summary feature was stopped.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

summary.description: Whether the AI Companion's meeting summary feature was stopped.

# meeting.registration_denied

The **Meeting Registration has been denied** event is triggered every time a registrant is denied from participating in a meeting.

A host with the Pro user type can enable the **Registration** feature when scheduling or updating a meeting. By [enabling registration for meetings](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/211579443-Registration-for-Meetings), participants must fill out a registration form receive information about joining the meeting.

A host can [manually reject](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360054446052-Managing-meeting-and-webinar-registration#h_01ETX8CE45EG3YT8S9JM32EW3Y) registrant. Hosts can also programmatically reject attendees via the [**Update registrant's status**](/docs/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/meetingRegistrantStatus) API.

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or a higher plan.
* The meeting host should be a user on your account or a user of your [webhook-enabled](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#enable-webhooks) app.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Meeting Registration has been denied** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:registrant:master`,`meeting:read:registrant`,`meeting:read:registrant:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.registration_denied`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

operator.description: The email address of the user who denied the meeting registration.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who denied the meeting registration.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

occurrences.description: Information about recurring meetings with fixed times and their occurrences. This only returns when the meeting's type value is 8.

# webinar.registration_denied

The **Webinar Registration has been denied** event is triggered every time one of your app users or account users denies a user's registration.

[Scheduling a Webinar with registration](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204619915-Scheduling-a-Webinar-with-Registration) requires registrants to complete a brief form before receiving the link to join the Webinar.[Webinar Registration options](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/202835649-Webinar-Registration-Customization#h_b74e0e26-5f3f-40d4-a3de-949dbfd85882) can be customized by a Zoom admin or a Webinar host. This customization allows them to choose whether to approve attendees automatically or manually.

When the registration is set to be approved manually, a pending registrant can be denied from joining the Webinar. To deny a registrant manually, navigate to your Webinars page and click on the name of the Webinar. Click **Edit** to the right of **Manage Attendees** option, select the user and click **Deny**. Alternatively, the registrant can be denied using the [**Update registrant's status**](/docs/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/webinarRegistrantStatus) API.

## Prerequisites
* Pro or a higher plan with Webinar license.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Webinar Registration has been denied** subscription enabled under the **Webinar** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:admin`,`webinar:write:admin`,`webinar:read`,`webinar:write`,`webinar:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:registrant:master`,`webinar:read:registrant`,`webinar:read:registrant:admin`

**Event type**: `webinar.registration_denied`

* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

# meeting.registration_approved

The **Meeting Registration has been approved** event is triggered every time a registrant is approved for the meeting. The meeting host should be a user on your account or a user of your [webhook-enabled](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#enable-webhooks) app.

A meeting host or a user with admin privileges can restrict meeting registrations by manually approving registrants. With the **Manually Approve** option enabled for [meeting registrations](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/211579443-Registration-for-Meetings), the host of the meeting must approve registrants before they receive information about how to join the meeting.

Meeting hosts can approve an attendee's meeting invitation via the [Zoom web portal](https://zoom.us). To do this, navigate to the scheduled meeting's page and click **Edit** next to the **Manage Attendees** section. Select the attendee to approve in the **Pending Approval** section, then click **Approve**.

To programmatically approve an attendee's registration, use the [**Update registrant's status**](/docs/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/meetingRegistrantStatus) API.

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or a higher plan.
* Enable **Manual Approval** for [meeting registrations](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/211579443-Registration-for-Meetings).
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Meeting Registration has been approved** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:registrant:master`,`meeting:read:registrant`,`meeting:read:registrant:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.registration_approved`

# webinar.participant_left

The **Participant/Host left webinar** event is triggered every time a host of a webinar or an attendee leaves the webinar. The host must be a user on your account or on other accounts that have installed your webhook-enabled app.

Zoom users with a [Webinar Plan](https://zoom.us/webinar) have access to creating and managing Webinars. [Zoom Video Webinar](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/200917029-Getting-Started-With-Webinar) allows users to broadcast a Zoom meeting to up to 1000 attendees.

## Prerequisites

* Pro or a higher plan with Webinar license.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Participant/Host left webinar** subscription enabled under the **Webinar** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:admin`,`webinar:write:admin`,`webinar:read`,`webinar:write`,`webinar:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:participant:master`,`webinar:read:participant`,`webinar:read:participant:admin`

**Event type**: `webinar.participant_left`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the webinar.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

type.description:
The webinar type. 
* 5 - A webinar. 
* 6 - A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 - A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

participant.description: Information about the webinar participant. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, email returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

user_id.description: The participant's webinar user ID. This value is assigned to a participant upon joining a webinar and is only valid for the duration of the webinar.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the webinar by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the webinar **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value. 

 This value returns blank for external users.

**Note:** Use the participant_user_id value instead of this value. We will remove this response in a future release.

leave_time.description: The time when the participant left the webinar.

leave_reason.description:
The reason why the participant left the webinar, where $name is the participant's username: 
* $name left the meeting. 
* $name got disconnected from the meeting. 
* Host ended the meeting. 
* Host closed the meeting. 
* Host started a new meeting. 
* Network connection error. 
* Host did not join. 
* Exceeded free meeting minutes limit. 
* Removed by host. 
* Unknown reason. 
* Leave waiting room. 
* Removed by host from waiting room.
x-enum: Removed by host from waiting room.

email.description: The participant's email address. This field is returned if the participant joined the webinar by logging into their Zoom account. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, this returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

registrant_id.description: The participant's registrant ID. (A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026)).

This value returns blank for external users.

participant_uuid.description: The participant's UUID for this specific webinar. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a webinar, and is only valid for the duration of that webinar.

customer_key.description: The participant's SDK identifier. This value can be alphanumeric.

phone_number.description: Phone number of participant joined via PSTN.

# recording.batch_deleted

The **Batch recording deleted** event is triggered every time one of your app users or account users permanently deletes one or more cloud recordings.

When a user initially deletes a recording, it is sent to the trash. At this stage, the recording can still be recovered for up to 30 days after deletion. To permanently delete a recording, users must delete it from the trash.

Users can permanently delete a recording via the [**Delete meeting recordings**](/api-reference/zoom-api/ma#operation/recordingDelete) API with the `delete` query parameter or in the [Zoom web portal](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205347605-Recording-Management).

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or higher plan.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
* The **Recording files have been permanently deleted in batches** subscription enabled under the **Recording** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `recording:read:admin`,`recording:write:admin`,`recording:read`,`recording:write`,`recording:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `cloud_recording:read:recording:master`,`cloud_recording:read:recording`,`cloud_recording:read:recording:admin`

**Event type**: `recording.batch_deleted`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user that completed the meeting or webinar recording.

operator.description: The email address of the user who deleted the recording.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who deleted the recording.

object.description: Information about the meeting or webinar recording.

items.description: Information about the meeting or webinar recording.

meeting_uuid.description: The universally unique identifier (UUID) of the recorded meeting or webinar instance.

recording_file_ids.description: The recording file's unique ID. If the current field value is NULL, it means to delete the whole Meeting Recording files.

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user that completed the meeting or webinar recording.

operator.description: The email address of the user who deleted the recording.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who deleted the recording.

object.description: Information about the meeting or webinar recording.

items.description: Information about the meeting or webinar recording.

meeting_uuid.description: The universally unique identifier (UUID) of the recorded meeting or webinar instance.

recording_file_ids.description: The recording file's unique ID. If the current field value is NULL, it means to delete the whole Meeting Recording files.

# webinar.alert

The **Webinar issues alert** event is triggered every time a Webinar hosted by one of your app users or account users, encounters a service issue.

Zoom monitors the Webinar statistics in regard to the quality of service for each Webinar. An account owner or a user with admin permissions on a Business or higher plan can view these statistics via Dashboard. The following quality metrics can trigger an alert:

* Unstable audio
* Unstable video
* Poor screen share quality
* High CPU usage
* Call reconnection problems

To receive notifications for this event, you must meet the following prerequisites.

## Prerequisites

*  Business or a higher plan.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Webinar issues alert** subscription enabled under the **Webinar** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:admin`,`webinar:write:admin`,`webinar:read`,`webinar:write`,`webinar:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:alert:master`,`webinar:read:alert`,`webinar:read:alert:admin`

**Event type**: `webinar.alert`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the webinar.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

type.description:
The webinar type: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

issues.description:
A list of issues that occurred during the webinar: 
* Unstable audio quality 
* Unstable video quality 
* Unstable screen share quality 
* High CPU occupation 
* Call Reconnection

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the webinar.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

type.description:
The webinar type: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

issues.description:
A list of issues that occurred during the webinar: 
* Unstable audio quality 
* Unstable video quality 
* Unstable screen share quality 
* High CPU occupation 
* Call Reconnection

# webinar.chat_message_sent

The **In-webinar chat message received** event is triggered when a user sends a public or private chat message during a webinar using the [chat feature](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205761999-Chatting-in-a-Zoom-Webinar). The event is sent to the configured endpoint in your Marketplace app.

## Prerequisites
* Have Zoom enable the DLP for in-meeting chat feature to use this webhook.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * Enable the **In-webinar chat message received** subscription under the **Webinar** event category.
* This feature is **only** enabled upon request approval. Contact the [Zoom support team](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362003) for more information.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:admin`,`webinar:write:admin`,`webinar:read`,`webinar:write`,`webinar:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:chat_message:master`,`webinar:read:chat_message`,`webinar:read:chat_message:admin`

**Event type**: `webinar.chat_message_sent`

event_ts.description: The timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the webinar.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

breakout_room_uuid.description: The breakout room's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each breakout room instance generates a breakout room UUID.

date_time.description: The date and time when the chat message was sent.

sender_email.description: The message sender's session email address.

sender_type.description:
The type of user that sent the message. 
* host - The webinar host. 
* alternative-host - The webinar's [alternative host](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/208220166). 
* panelist - A webinar [panelist](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360000252726#:~:text=Panelists%20are%20full,a%20webinar%20host.). 
* guest - A webinar guest.

sender_context.description:
The context where the sender is located when the message is generated. 
* webinar - The sender is in a webinar. 
* breakout_room - The sender is in a breakout room. 
* waiting_room - The sender is in the waiting room.

recipient_email.description: The message recipient's email address. Optional only included if it is a private message.

recipient_type.description:
The type of message recipient. 
* everyone - All webinar participants. 
* host - The webinar host. 
* guest - A webinar guest. 
* group - A group chat. 
* backstage - The webinar backstage. 
* host_group - The webinar host and all panelists.

recipient_context.description:
The context where the recipient is located when the message is delivered. 
* webinar - The recipient is in the webinar. 
* breakout_room - The recipient is in a breakout room. 
* waiting_room - The recipient is in the waiting room.

message_content.description: The content of the chat message.

file_ids.description: The live webinar chat file's UUID, in base64-encoded format.

# meeting.ended

The **End Meeting** event is triggered every time a meeting host ends the meeting. The host must be a user in your account or a user in any other accounts that have installed your [webhook-enabled](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#enable-webhooks) app.

## Prerequisites

* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **End Meeting** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:meeting:master`,`meeting:read:meeting`,`meeting:read:meeting:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.ended`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

end_time.description: The date and time at which the meeting ended.

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

end_time.description: The date and time at which the meeting ended.

# meeting.participant_joined_breakout_room

The **Meeting participant has joined a breakout room** event is triggered when a meeting participant joins a meeting [breakout room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115005769646-Participating-in-breakout-rooms).

Breakout rooms are sessions that are split off from the main Zoom meeting. They let participants meet in smaller groups and are completely isolated in terms of audio and video from the main session.

## Prerequisites

* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Meeting participant has joined a breakout room** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant:master`,`meeting:read:participant`,`meeting:read:participant:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.participant_joined_breakout_room`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the main meeting.

uuid.description: The main meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

breakout_room_uuid.description: The breakout room's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each breakout room instance generates a breakout room UUID.

host_id.description: The main meeting host's user ID.

type.description:
The main meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

user_id.description: The participant's meeting user ID. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

parent_user_id.description: The participant's main meeting user ID (user_id).

id.description:
The participant's unique ID. 
 * If meeting registration was **not** required and the participant joined by logging into Zoom, this value is the same as the userId field used in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API. 
* If registration was required for the meeting and the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the same as the id value in the [**List meeting registrants**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/meetingRegistrants) API response. 
* If participant joins without logging into Zoom, this returns an empty value. 

 This value returns blank for external users.

**Note:** Use the participant_user_id value instead of this value. We will remove this response in a future release.

participant_uuid.description: The participant's UUID for this specific meeting and any breakout rooms created in this meeting. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of that meeting.

join_time.description: The time when the participant joined the meeting.

email.description: The participant's email address. This response only returns if the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom.

registrant_id.description: The participant's registrant ID. A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026).

participant_user_id.description:

* If participant joins without logging into Zoom, this returns an empty value. 

This value returns blank for external users.
