# meeting.participant_left_waiting_room

The **Participant left a waiting room** event is triggered every time a meeting host **does not** admit a participant to the meeting and **removes** the participant from the waiting room. This event also triggers if a participant leaves the waiting room. The meeting host must be a user in your account or a user in any other accounts that have installed your [webhook-enabled](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#enable-webhooks) app.

The [**waiting room**](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115000332726-Waiting-Room) feature allows the host to control when participants join a meeting. When a participant attempts to join a meeting with this feature enabled, the participant is first admitted to the waiting room.
The meeting host can then admit attendees one by one or hold all attendees in the waiting room and admit them all at once.

## Prerequisites

* The [**waiting room**](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115000332726-Waiting-Room) feature enabled for the meeting.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The "**Participant left a waiting room**" subscription must be enabled under the **Meeting** event.





**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant:master`,`meeting:read:participant`,`meeting:read:participant:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.participant_left_waiting_room`

# meeting.started

The **Start Meeting** event is triggered every time a meeting host starts a meeting. The meeting host must either be a user on your account or a user of your [webhook-enabled](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#enable-webhooks) app.

A meeting host can [start a meeting](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362423-How-do-I-Start-or-Join-a-Scheduled-Meeting-as-the-Host-) from the Zoom desktop client for Mac, PC or Linux, the Zoom app for Android or iOS, a web browser, or an H.323 or SIP device.

## Prerequisites

* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Start Meeting** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:meeting:master`,`meeting:read:meeting`,`meeting:read:meeting:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.started`

* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

# meeting.registration_cancelled

The **Meeting Registration has been canceled** event is triggered every time a registration is canceled for a meeting. The meeting host should be a user on your account or a user of your [webhook-enabled](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#enable-webhooks) app.

A host with the Pro user type can enable the **Registration** feature when scheduling or updating a meeting. By [enabling registration for meetings](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/211579443-Registration-for-Meetings), participants must fill out a registration form receive information about joining the meeting.

Meeting attendees can cancel their registration at any time. A meeting host can also cancel an attendee's meeting invitation via the [Zoom web portal](https://zoom.us). To do this, navigate to the scheduled meeting's page and click **Edit** next to the **Manage Attendees** section. The **Approved** section will display all approved attendees. Select the attendee whose meeting registration you want to revoke and click **Cancel Registration**.

To programmatically cancel a registration, use the [**Update registrant's status**](/docs/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/meetingRegistrantStatus) API.

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or a higher plan.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Meeting Registration has been canceled** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:registrant:master`,`meeting:read:registrant`,`meeting:read:registrant:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.registration_cancelled`

* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

# meeting.summary_completed

The **Meeting summary has been completed** event is triggered every time the summary of the meeting is made available for one of your app users or account users after the summarized meeting/webinar ends.

## Prerequisites

* Host user must be Pro or higher plan.
* Enable the **Meeting Summary with AI Companion** feature in the host's account.
* E2ee meetings do not have summary feature enabled.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Meeting summary have completed** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting_summary:read:admin`,`meeting_summary:read`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:summary:master`,`meeting:read:summary`,`meeting:read:summary:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.summary_completed`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who hosted the meeting.

meeting_host_id.description: The ID of the user who is set as the meeting host.

meeting_uuid.description: Unique meeting ID. Each meeting instance generates its own meeting UUID - after a meeting ends, a new UUID is generated for the next instance of the meeting. Retrieve a list of UUIDs from past meeting instances using the [**List past meeting instances**](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/#operation/pastMeetings) API. [Double encode](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#meeting-id-and-uuid) your UUID when using it for API calls if the UUID begins with a / or contains // in it.

meeting_id.description: [Meeting ID](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362373-What-is-a-Meeting-ID-) - the meeting's unique identifier in **long** format, represented as int64 data type in JSON. Also known as the meeting number.

meeting_start_time.description: The meeting's start date and time.

meeting_end_time.description: The meeting's end date and time.

summary_start_time.description: The summary's start date and time.

summary_end_time.description: The summary's end date and time.

summary_created_time.description: The date and time when the meeting summary was created.

summary_last_modified_time.description: The date and time when the meeting summary was last modified.

summary_content.description: The complete meeting summary in Markdown format. This unified field is used for all summaries. For compatibility, the legacy fields summary_overview, summary_details, next_steps are still returned, but are deprecated and will not be supported in the future.

summary_doc_url.description: The URL to view the full summary document in Zoom Docs.

{
  "event": "meeting.summary_completed",
  "event_ts": 1626230691572,
  "payload": {
    "account_id": "dzVA4QmMQfyISoRcpFO8CA",
    "object": {
      "meeting_host_id": "30R7kT7bTIKSNUFEuH_Qlg",
      "meeting_host_email": "jchill@example.com",
      "meeting_uuid": "aDYlohsHRtCd4ii1uC2+hA==",
      "meeting_id": 97763643886,
      "meeting_topic": "My Meeting",
      "meeting_start_time": "2019-07-15T23:24:52Z",
      "meeting_end_time": "2020-07-15T23:30:19Z",
      "summary_start_time": "2019-07-15T23:24:52Z",
      "summary_end_time": "2020-07-15T23:30:19Z",
      "summary_created_time": "2019-07-15T23:24:52Z",
      "summary_last_modified_time": "2020-07-15T23:30:19Z",
      "summary_title": "Meeting Summary for my meeting",
      "summary_overview": "Meeting Overview",
      "summary_details": [
        {
          "label": "Meeting Overview",
          "summary": "Meeting Overview"
        }
      ],
      "next_steps": [
        "step1"
      ],
      "summary_content": "## Key takeaways
- Mobile app performance issues are affecting user retention.
- New onboarding flow received positive feedback from beta testers.
- Need to prioritize accessibility improvements.
- Customer support response time has improved by 25%.

## Discussed topics
### Mobile App Performance
Discussion of recent performance metrics and user complaints
- **Details**
    - Sarah (Product): Reports of app crashes increased 15% this month
    - Mike (Engineering): Memory optimization needed in latest release
    - Tom (QA): Identified memory leak in photo upload feature
- **Conclusion**
    - Implement performance monitoring tools
    - Prioritize memory optimization in next sprint

### Onboarding Flow
Review of beta testing results for new user onboarding
- **Details**
    - Rachel (UX): 90% completion rate in beta testing
    - David (Product): Positive feedback on simplified registration
- **Conclusion**
    - Ready for full rollout next month
    - Need to monitor analytics post-launch

### Accessibility Compliance
Discussion of current accessibility status and needed improvements
- **Details**
    - Lisa (Design): Screen reader compatibility issues identified
    - John (Engineering): WCAG compliance at 80%
- **Conclusion**
    - Create accessibility improvement roadmap
    - Schedule external audit

## Challenges
* Resource constraints for performance optimization
* Integration testing environment stability issues
* Lack of accessibility expertise in the team

## Action items
- **Sarah**
  - Prepare performance monitoring implementation plan
  - Schedule follow-up meeting with engineering team
- **Mike**
  - Investigate memory leak fix
  - Document performance optimization guidelines
- **Lisa**
  - Create accessibility improvement proposal
  - Research accessibility testing tools
- **Rachel**
  - Prepare onboarding analytics dashboard
  - Document beta testing findings",
      "summary_doc_url": "https://docs.example.com/doc/1aBcDeFgHiJkLmNoPqRsTu"
    }
  }
}

- New onboarding flow received positive feedback from beta testers.
- Need to prioritize accessibility improvements.
- Customer support response time has improved by 25%.

## Discussed topics
### Mobile App Performance
Discussion of recent performance metrics and user complaints
- **Details**
    - Sarah (Product): Reports of app crashes increased 15% this month
    - Mike (Engineering): Memory optimization needed in latest release
    - Tom (QA): Identified memory leak in photo upload feature
- **Conclusion**
    - Implement performance monitoring tools
    - Prioritize memory optimization in next sprint

### Onboarding Flow
Review of beta testing results for new user onboarding
- **Details**
    - Rachel (UX): 90% completion rate in beta testing
    - David (Product): Positive feedback on simplified registration
- **Conclusion**
    - Ready for full rollout next month
    - Need to monitor analytics post-launch

### Accessibility Compliance
Discussion of current accessibility status and needed improvements
- **Details**
    - Lisa (Design): Screen reader compatibility issues identified
    - John (Engineering): WCAG compliance at 80%
- **Conclusion**
    - Create accessibility improvement roadmap
    - Schedule external audit

## Challenges
* Resource constraints for performance optimization
* Integration testing environment stability issues
* Lack of accessibility expertise in the team

## Action items
- **Sarah**
  - Prepare performance monitoring implementation plan
  - Schedule follow-up meeting with engineering team
- **Mike**
  - Investigate memory leak fix
  - Document performance optimization guidelines
- **Lisa**
  - Create accessibility improvement proposal
  - Research accessibility testing tools
- **Rachel**
  - Prepare onboarding analytics dashboard
  - Document beta testing findings",
      "summary_doc_url": "https://docs.example.com/doc/1aBcDeFgHiJkLmNoPqRsTu"
    }
  }
}

# meeting.participant_left

The **Participant/Host left meeting** event is triggered every time an attendee leaves a meeting. A meeting attendee is a meeting participant or the host. The meeting host must be a user in your account or a user in any other accounts that have installed your [webhook-enabled](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#enable-webhooks) app.

## Prerequisites

* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with these configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Participant/Host left meeting** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant:master`,`meeting:read:participant`,`meeting:read:participant:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.participant_left`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

participant.description: Information about the meeting participant. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, email returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

user_id.description: The participant's meeting user ID. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value. 

 This value returns blank for external users.

**Note:** Use the participant_user_id value instead of this value. We will remove this response in a future release.

participant_uuid.description: The participant's UUID for this specific meeting and any breakout rooms created in this meeting. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of that meeting.

leave_time.description: The time when the participant left the meeting.

leave_reason.description:
The reason why the participant left the meeting, where $name is the participant's username: 
* $name left the meeting. 
* $name got disconnected from the meeting. 
* Host ended the meeting. 
* Host closed the meeting. 
* Host started a new meeting. 
* Network connection error. 
* Host did not join. 
* Exceeded free meeting minutes limit. 
* Removed by host. 
* Unknown reason. 
* Leave waiting room. 
* Removed by host from waiting room.
x-enum: Removed by host from waiting room.

email.description: The participant's email address. This response only returns if the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, this returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

registrant_id.description: The participant's registrant ID. (A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026)).

participant_user_id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

This value returns blank for external users.

customer_key.description: The participant's SDK identifier. This value can be alphanumeric.

phone_number.description: Phone number of participant joined via PSTN

{ "event": "meeting.participant_left", "event_ts": 1626230691572, "payload": { "account_id": "AAAAAABBBB", "object": { "id": "1234567890", "uuid": "4444AAAiAAAAAiAiAiiAii==", "host_id": "x1yCzABCDEfg23HiJKl4mN", "topic": "My Meeting", "type": 8, "start_time": "2021-07-13T21:44:51Z", "timezone": "America/Los_Angeles", "duration": 60, "participant": { "user_id": "1234567890", "user_name": "Jill Chill", "id": "iFxeBPYun6SAiWUzBcEkX", "participant_uuid": "55555AAAiAAAAAiAiAiiAii", "leave_time": "2021-07-13T22:50:51Z", "leave_reason": "Jill Chill left the meeting.
Reason: Host ended the meeting.", "date_time": "2021-07-13T21:44:51Z", "email": "jchill@example.com", "registrant_id": "abcdefghij0-klmnopq23456", "participant_user_id": "rstuvwxyza789-cde", "customer_key": "349589LkJyeW", "phone_number": "8615250064084" } } } }

* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value. 

 This value returns blank for external users.

**Note:** Use the participant_user_id value instead of this value. We will remove this response in a future release.

leave_time.description: The time when the participant left the meeting.

leave_reason.description:
The reason why the participant left the meeting, where $name is the participant's username: 
* $name left the meeting. 
* $name got disconnected from the meeting. 
* Host ended the meeting. 
* Host closed the meeting. 
* Host started a new meeting. 
* Network connection error. 
* Host did not join. 
* Exceeded free meeting minutes limit.

* Leave waiting room. 
* Removed by host from waiting room.
x-enum: Removed by host from waiting room.

If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, this returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

registrant_id.description: The participant's registrant ID. (A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026)).

participant_user_id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

This value returns blank for external users.

customer_key.description: The participant's SDK identifier. This value can be alphanumeric.

phone_number.description: Phone number of participant joined via PSTN

json-example.value:
{ "event": "meeting.participant_left", "event_ts": 1626230691572, "payload": { "account_id": "AAAAAABBBB", "object": { "id": "1234567890", "uuid": "4444AAAiAAAAAiAiAiiAii==", "host_id": "x1yCzABCDEfg23HiJKl4mN", "topic": "My Meeting", "type": 8, "start_time": "2021-07-13T21:44:51Z", "timezone": "America/Los_Angeles", "duration": 60, "participant": { "user_id": "1234567890", "user_name": "Jill Chill", "id": "iFxeBPYun6SAiWUzBcEkX", "participant_uuid": "55555AAAiAAAAAiAiAiiAii", "leave_time": "2021-07-13T22:50:51Z", "leave_reason": "Jill Chill left the meeting.
Reason: Host ended the meeting.", "date_time": "2021-07-13T21:44:51Z", "email": "jchill@example.com", "registrant_id": "abcdefghij0-klmnopq23456", "participant_user_id": "rstuvwxyza789-cde", "customer_key": "349589LkJyeW", "phone_number": "8615250064084" } } } }

# meeting.participant_phone_callout_rejected

The **Invited party rejected a meeting invitation through phone (call out)** event is triggered every time a user rejects an invitation to [join a meeting through phone (call out)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/4404535651085).

## Prerequisites

Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with these configurations.
* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
* The **Invited party rejected a meeting invitation through phone (call out)** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant_callout:master`,`meeting:read:participant_callout`,`meeting:read:participant_callout:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.participant_phone_callout_rejected`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The meeting host's account ID.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

invitee_name.description: The user's name to display in the meeting.

phone_number.description: The user's phone number.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The meeting host's account ID.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

invitee_name.description: The user's name to display in the meeting.

phone_number.description: The user's phone number.

# webinar.sharing_started

The **Webinar Sharing Started** event is triggered every time one of your app users or account users share their screen during a Webinar.

Zoom supports [screen sharing](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362153-Screen-Sharing-and-Annotations) by host or panelists on desktop, tablet and mobile devices running Zoom during Webinars.

## Prerequisites

* Pro or a higher plan with Webinar license.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Webinar Sharing Started** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:admin`,`webinar:write:admin`,`webinar:read`,`webinar:write`,`webinar:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:participant_sharing:master`,`webinar:read:participant_sharing`,`webinar:read:participant_sharing:admin`

**Event type**: `webinar.sharing_started`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the webinar.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

type.description:
The webinar type: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

user_id.description: The participant's webinar user ID. This value is assigned to a participant upon joining a webinar and is only valid for the duration of the webinar.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID): 
* If the participant joins the webinar by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the webinar **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

content.description:
The type of shared content: 
* application — An application. For example, a web browser. 
* whiteboard — The [classic whiteboard](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205677665-Sharing-a-whiteboard). 
* desktop — A user's desktop. 
* airplay — A user's [AirPlay (Apple screen mirroring)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204726885-Sharing-screen-using-Apple-screen-mirroring-AirPlay-). 
* camera — A user's camera. 
* unknown — An unrecognized application, such as a third party app.

link_source.description:
The Dropbox sharing method: 
* deep_link — A [deep link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_linking). 
* in_meeting — A link shared via Zoom. 
* An empty string value if the link source is **not** a Dropbox link.

file_link.description: The Dropbox file's link. This field returns an empty string value if the file link is **not** from a Dropbox source.

date_time.description: The screenshare's date and time.

source.description:
The source from which the file was shared: 
* dropbox — A Dropbox source. 
* An empty string value if the source is **not** Dropbox.

* If the participant joins the webinar **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

* camera — A user's camera. 
* unknown — An unrecognized application, such as a third party app.

The Dropbox sharing method: 
* deep_link — A [deep link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_linking). 
* in_meeting — A link shared via Zoom. 
* An empty string value if the link source is **not** a Dropbox link.

* dropbox — A Dropbox source. 
* An empty string value if the source is **not** Dropbox.

# meeting.registration_created

The **Meeting Registration has been created** event is triggered every time a user [registers](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/211579443-Registration-for-Meetings) for a meeting.

A host with the Pro user type can enable the **Registration** feature when scheduling or updating a meeting. By [enabling registration for meetings](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/211579443-Registration-for-Meetings), participants must fill out a registration form receive information about joining the meeting. You can also programmatically register a participant via the [**Add Meeting Registrant**](/docs/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/meetingRegistrantCreate) API.

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or a higher plan.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Meeting Registration has been created** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:registrant:master`,`meeting:read:registrant`,`meeting:read:registrant:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.registration_created`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

occurrences.description: Information about recurring meetings with fixed times and their occurrences. This only returns when the meeting's type value is 8.

zip.description: The registrant's ZIP or postal code.

purchasing_time_frame.description:
The registrant's purchasing time frame: 
* Within a month 
* 1-3 months 
* 4-6 months 
* More than 6 months 
* No timeframe 
* An empty string. — No purchasing time frame set.

role_in_purchase_process.description:
The registrant's role in the purchase process: 
* Decision Maker 
* Evaluator/Recommender 
* Influencer 
* Not involved 
* An empty string. — No role set.

no_of_employees.description:
The registrant's number of employees: 
* 1-20 
* 21-50 
* 51-100 
* 101-250 
* 251-500 
* 501-1,000 
* 1,001-5,000 
* 5,001-10,000 
* More than 10,000 
* An empty string. — No employee number set.

title.description: The title of the custom question.

value.description: The custom question's response value. This has a limit of 128 characters.

status.description:
The registrant's registration approval status: 
* approve — Approved registrant. 
* pending — Registration approval pending.

join_url.description: The URL to join the meeting.

participant_pin_code.description: The participant PIN code is used to authenticate audio participants before they join the meeting.

json-example.value:
{
  "event": "meeting.registration_created",
  "event_ts": 1626230691572,
  "payload": {
    "account_id": "AAAAAABBBB",
    "object": {
      "uuid": "4444AAAiAAAAAiAiAiiAii==",
      "id": 1234567890,
      "host_id": "x1yCzABCDEfg23HiJKl4mN",
      "topic": "My Meeting",
      "type": 8,
      "start_time": "2021-07-13T21:44:51Z",
      "duration": 60,
      "timezone": "America/Los_Angeles",
      "occurrences": [
        {
          "occurrence_id": "ABCDE12345",
          "start_time": "2021-07-13T21:44:51Z"
        }
      ],
      "registrant": {
        "id": "iFxeBPYun6SAiWUzBcEkX",
        "first_name": "Jill",
        "last_name": "Chill",
        "email": "jchill@example.com",
        "address": "1800 Amphibious Blvd.",
        "city": "Mountain View",
        "country": "US",
        "zip": "94045",
        "state": "CA",
        "phone": "5550100",
        "industry": "Food",
        "org": "Cooking Org",
        "job_title": "Chef",
        "purchasing_time_frame": "1-3 months",
        "role_in_purchase_process": "Influencer",
        "no_of_employees": "1-20",
        "comments": "Looking forward to the meeting",
        "custom_questions": [
          {
            "title": "What do you hope to learn from this meeting?",
            "value": "Look forward to learning how you come up with new recipes and what other services you offer."
          }
        ],
        "status": "approved",
        "join_url": "https://example.com/join",
        "participant_pin_code": 380303
      }
    }
  }
}

* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting.

# recording.recovered

This event is triggered when a user recovers a recording from the trash.

Zoom allows users to recover their cloud recordings from the trash within 30 days of deletion. You can recover a deleted cloud recording via the [**Recordings**](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/216378863-Recover-a-Deleted-Cloud-Recording) tab in the Zoom web client or via the [**Recover meeting recordings**](/api-reference/zoom-api/ma#operation/recordingStatusUpdate) API.

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or a higher plan.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Recording files have been recovered from trash** subscription enabled under the **Recording** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `recording:read:admin`,`recording:write:admin`,`recording:read`,`recording:write`,`recording:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `cloud_recording:read:recording:master`,`cloud_recording:read:recording`,`cloud_recording:read:recording:admin`

**Event type**: `recording.recovered`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

download_token.description:
Use the generated token value with the download_url value to download the cloud recording via an [OAuth app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/getting-started/app-types/create-oauth-app). The download token only lasts for 24 hours after its creation and you can only download the file within 24 hours of receiving the event notification. You can either include the download_token as a query parameter or pass it as a Bearer token in the Authorization header of your HTTP request: 

 **Using an Authorization header (Recommended)** 

 
curl --request GET \
  --url {download_url} \
  --header 'authorization: Bearer {download_token} \
  --header 'content-type: application/json'
 

**Using a query parameter** 

 
{download_url}/?access_token={download_token}
 

 For example: https://zoom.us/recording/download/123456?access_token=abcdefgh

account_id.description: The account ID of the user that completed the meeting or webinar recording.

operator.description: The email address of the user who recovered the recording.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who recovered the recording.

object.description: Information about the meeting or webinar recording.

id.description: The ID of the recorded meeting (meetingId) or webinar (webinarId).

uuid.description: The universally unique identifier (UUID) of the recorded meeting or webinar instance.

host_id.description: The ID of the user set as the host of the meeting or webinar.

type.description:
The type of recorded meeting or webinar: 

 If the recording is of a meeting: 
* 1 — Instant meeting. 
* 2 — Scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A meeting created via PMI (Personal Meeting ID). 
* 7 — A [Personal Audio Conference](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204517069-Getting-Started-with-Personal-Audio-Conference) (PAC). 
* 8 - Recurring meeting with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is of a webinar: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is **not** from a meeting or webinar: 

* 99 — A recording uploaded via the [**Recordings**](https://zoom.us/recording) interface on the Zoom Web Portal.

start_time.description: The meeting or webinar's start time.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user that completed the meeting or webinar recording.

duration.description: The meeting or webinar's scheduled duration.

share_url.description: The URL at which approved users can view the recording.

total_size.description: The recording file's total size, in bytes.

recording_count.description: The number of recovered recording files.

recording_files.description: Information about the recovered recording files.

meeting_id.description: The meeting's ID.

recording_start.description: The date and time at which recording started.

recording_end.description: The date and time at which recording ended.

file_type.description: The file type of the recording file.

file_size.description: The recording file's size, in bytes.

file_extension.description: The file extension type of the recording file.

play_url.description: The URL at which the recording file can be opened and played.

download_url.description:
The URL at which to download the the recording. 

 **JWT apps** 

 To access a private or password-protected cloud recording of a user in your account, use a [Zoom JWT app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/getting-started/app-types/create-jwt-app). Use the generated JWT token as the value of the access_token query parameter and include this query parameter at the end of the URL. 

 https://{{base-domain}}/recording/download/{{path-to-file-download}}?access_token={{JWT-token}} 

 **OAuth apps** 

 If a user has authorized and installed your OAuth app that contains recording scopes, use the user's [OAuth access token](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth/) to download the file. For example: 

 https://{{base-domain}}/rec/archive/download/xxx?access_token={{OAuth-access-token}}.  

**Note:** We recommend that you send the access_token as a Bearer token in the Authorization header, for example: "Authorization": "Bearer <ACCESS_TOKEN>”.

status.description:
The recording file's processing status: 
* completed — The processing of the file is complete. 
* processing — The file is processing.

recording_type.description:
The recording file type. 
* shared_screen_with_speaker_view(CC) 
* shared_screen_with_speaker_view 
* shared_screen_with_gallery_view 
* gallery_view 
* shared_screen 
* audio_only 
* audio_transcript 
* chat_file 
* active_speaker 
* host_video 
* audio_only_each_participant 
* cc_transcript 
* closed_caption 
* poll 
* timeline 
* thumbnail 
* audio_interpretation 
* summary 
* summary_next_steps 
* summary_smart_chapters 
*sign_interpretation 
*production_studio 

For more information, read our [Managing and sharing cloud recordings](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205347605-Managing-and-sharing-cloud-recordings#h_9898497b-e736-4980-a749-d55608f10773) documentation.

file_size.description: The size of the recording file, in bytes.

# meeting.sharing_ended

The **Meeting Sharing Ended** event is triggered every time an attendee or a host stops sharing their screen during a Zoom meeting. The meeting host must be a user in your account or a user in any other accounts that have installed your [webhook-enabled](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#enable-webhooks) app.

Zoom allows [screen sharing](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362153-Screen-Sharing-and-Annotations) by host and attendees on desktop, tablet, and mobile devices running Zoom during meetings.

## Prerequisites

* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Meeting Sharing Ended** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant_sharing:master`,`meeting:read:participant_sharing`,`meeting:read:participant_sharing:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.sharing_ended`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

user_id.description: The participant's meeting user ID. This value is assigned to a participant upon joining a meeting and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID): 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

content.description:
The type of shared content: 
* application — An application. For example, a web browser. 
* whiteboard — The [classic whiteboard](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205677665-Sharing-a-whiteboard). 
* desktop — A user's desktop. 
* airplay — A user's [AirPlay (Apple screen mirroring)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204726885-Sharing-screen-using-Apple-screen-mirroring-AirPlay-). 
* camera — A user's camera. 
* unknown — An unrecognized application, such as a third party app.

link_source.description:
The Dropbox sharing method: 
* deep_link — A [deep link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_linking). 
* in_meeting — A link shared via Zoom. 
* An empty (””) value if the link source is **not** a Dropbox link.

file_link.description: The Dropbox file's link. This field returns an empty (””) value if the file link is **not** from a Dropbox source.

date_time.description: The screenshare's date and time.

source.description:
The source from which the file was shared: 
* dropbox — A Dropbox source. 
* An empty (””) value if the source is **not** Dropbox.

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

* desktop — A user's desktop. 
* airplay — A user's [AirPlay (Apple screen mirroring)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204726885-Sharing-screen-using-Apple-screen-mirroring-AirPlay-). 
* camera — A user's camera. 
* unknown — An unrecognized application, such as a third party app.

# recording.cloud_storage_usage_updated

The **Recording Cloud Storage Usage Updated** event is triggered every time storage usage changes.

States such as recording completed, trashed, or recovered trigger this event.

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or higher plan.
* Enable Cloud recording on the users' account.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Recording Cloud Storage Usage Updated** subscription enabled under the **Recording** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `recording:read:admin`,`recording:write:admin`,`recording:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `cloud_recording:read:recording:admin`

**Event type**: `recording.cloud_storage_usage_updated`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the host who recorded the webinar.

max_exceed_date.description: The maximum date that overage storage.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the host who recorded the webinar.

max_exceed_date.description: The maximum date that overage storage.

# recording.transcript_completed

The **Recording transcript files have completed** event is triggered every time the transcript of the recording is made available for one of your app users or account users after the recorded meeting/webinar ends.

Zoom supports audio transcription of cloud recordings. To utilize audio transcription, users must first [enable](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115004794983-Automatically-Transcribe-Cloud-Recordings) it. An account owner or a user with admin permissions can enable cloud recording for an account or a group of users.

## Prerequisites

* A Business, Education, or Enterprise license.
* Cloud recording must be enabled on the users' account.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Recording transcript files have completed** subscription enabled under the **Recording** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `recording:read:admin`,`recording:write:admin`,`recording:read`,`recording:write`,`recording:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `cloud_recording:read:recording:master`,`cloud_recording:read:recording`,`cloud_recording:read:recording:admin`

**Event type**: `recording.transcript_completed`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

download_token.description:
Use the generated token value with the download_url value to download the cloud recording via an [OAuth app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/getting-started/app-types/create-oauth-app). The download token only lasts for 24 hours after its creation and you can only download the file within 24 hours of receiving the event notification. You can either include the download_token as a query parameter or pass it as a Bearer token in the Authorization header of your HTTP request: 

 **Using an Authorization header (Recommended)** 

 
curl --request GET \
  --url {download_url} \
  --header 'authorization: Bearer {download_token} \
  --header 'content-type: application/json'
 

**Using a query parameter** 

 
{download_url}/?access_token={download_token}
 

 For example: https://zoom.us/recording/download/123456?access_token=abcdefgh

account_id.description: The account ID of the user that hosted the meeting or the webinar.

object.description: Information about the webinar or meeting recording.

id.description: The ID of the meeting (meetingId) or the webinar (webinarId).

uuid.description: The universally unique identifier (UUID) of the recorded meeting or webinar instance. Each meeting or webinar instance generates its own meeting UUID.

host_id.description: The ID of the user set as the host of the archived meeting or webinar.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user that ended the meeting and completed the recording.

type.description:
The type of recorded meeting or webinar: 

 If the recording is of a meeting: 
* 1 — Instant meeting. 
* 2 — Scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A meeting created via PMI (Personal Meeting ID). 
* 7 — A [Personal Audio Conference](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204517069-Getting-Started-with-Personal-Audio-Conference) (PAC). 
* 8 - Recurring meeting with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is of a webinar: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is **not** from a meeting or webinar: 

* 99 — A recording uploaded via the [**Recordings**](https://zoom.us/recording) interface on the Zoom Web Portal.

start_time.description: The meeting or webinar's start time.

duration.description: The meeting or webinar's scheduled duration.

share_url.description: The URL at which approved users can view the recording.

total_size.description: The recording file's total size, in bytes.

recording_count.description: The number of completed recording files.

recording_files.description: Information about the completed recording files.

meeting_id.description: The meeting's ID.

recording_start.description: The date and time at which recording started.

recording_end.description: The date and time at which recording ended.

file_type.description: The recording file's type.

file_size.description: The recording file's size, in bytes.

play_url.description: The URL at which the recording file can be opened and played.

download_url.description:
The URL at which to download the the recording. 

 **JWT apps** 

 To access a private or password-protected cloud recording of a user in your account, use a [Zoom JWT app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/getting-started/app-types/create-jwt-app). Use the generated JWT token as the value of the access_token query parameter and include this query parameter at the end of the URL. 

 https://{{base-domain}}/recording/download/{{path-to-file-download}}?access_token={{JWT-token}} 

 **OAuth apps** 

 If a user has authorized and installed your OAuth app that contains recording scopes, use the user's [OAuth access token](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth/) to download the file. For example: 

 https://{{base-domain}}/rec/archive/download/xxx?access_token={{OAuth-access-token}} 

 **Note:** This field does **not** return for [Zoom On-Premise accounts](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360034064852-Zoom-On-Premise-Deployment). Instead, this API will return the file_path field.

file_path.description:
The file path to the On-Premise account recording. 

 **Note:** This API returns this field for [Zoom On-Premise accounts](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360034064852-Zoom-On-Premise-Deployment). It does **not** return the download_url field.

status.description:
The recording file's processing status: 
* completed — The processing of the file is complete. 
* processing — The file is processing.

recording_type.description:
The type of recording file: 
* shared_screen_with_speaker_view(CC) 
* shared_screen_with_speaker_view 
* shared_screen_with_gallery_view 
* gallery_view 
* shared_screen 
* audio_only 
* audio_transcript 
* chat_file 
* active_speaker 
* host_video 
* audio_only_each_participant 
* cc_transcript 
* closed_caption 
* poll 
* timeline 
* thumbnail 

For more information, read our [Managing and sharing cloud recordings](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205347605-Managing-and-sharing-cloud-recordings#h_9898497b-e736-4980-a749-d55608f10773) documentation.

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

download_token.description:
Use the generated token value with the download_url value to download the cloud recording via an [OAuth app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/getting-started/app-types/create-oauth-app). The download token only lasts for 24 hours after its creation and you can only download the file within 24 hours of receiving the event notification. You can either include the download_token as a query parameter or pass it as a Bearer token in the Authorization header of your HTTP request: 

 **Using an Authorization header (Recommended)** 

 
curl --request GET \
  --url {download_url} \
  --header 'authorization: Bearer {download_token} \
  --header 'content-type: application/json'
 

**Using a query parameter** 

 
{download_url}/?access_token={download_token}
 

 For example: https://zoom.us/recording/download/123456?access_token=abcdefgh

object.description: Information about the webinar or meeting recording.

id.description: The ID of the meeting (meetingId) or the webinar (webinarId).

host_id.description: The ID of the user set as the host of the archived meeting or webinar.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user that ended the meeting and completed the recording.

type.description:
The type of recorded meeting or webinar: 

 If the recording is of a meeting: 
* 1 — Instant meeting. 
* 2 — Scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A meeting created via PMI (Personal Meeting ID). 
* 7 — A [Personal Audio Conference](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204517069-Getting-Started-with-Personal-Audio-Conference) (PAC). 
* 8 - Recurring meeting with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is of a webinar: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is **not** from a meeting or webinar: 

* 99 — A recording uploaded via the [**Recordings**](https://zoom.us/recording) interface on the Zoom Web Portal.

play_url.description: The URL at which the recording file can be opened and played.

download_url.description:
The URL at which to download the the recording. 

 **JWT apps** 

 To access a private or password-protected cloud recording of a user in your account, use a [Zoom JWT app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/getting-started/app-types/create-jwt-app). Use the generated JWT token as the value of the access_token query parameter and include this query parameter at the end of the URL. 

 https://{{base-domain}}/recording/download/{{path-to-file-download}}?access_token={{JWT-token}} 

 **OAuth apps** 

 If a user has authorized and installed your OAuth app that contains recording scopes, use the user's [OAuth access token](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth/) to download the file. For example: 

 https://{{base-domain}}/rec/archive/download/xxx?access_token={{OAuth-access-token}} 

 **Note:** This field does **not** return for [Zoom On-Premise accounts](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360034064852-Zoom-On-Premise-Deployment). Instead, this API will return the file_path field.

file_path.description:

 **Note:** This API returns this field for [Zoom On-Premise accounts](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360034064852-Zoom-On-Premise-Deployment). It does **not** return the download_url field.

status.description:

* processing — The file is processing.

# recording.stopped

The **Recording Stopped** event is triggered every time a recording is stopped by one of your app's users or account users.

All users with a Pro or a higher plan have access to [cloud recordings](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203741855-Cloud-Recording). An account owner or account admin can [enable cloud recording](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203741855-Cloud-Recording#h_29fc1846-190d-4f05-ab23-1070c8106ec8) for all account members as well as for members that belong to a certain group. Users can also enable cloud recordings [for their own use](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203741855-Cloud-Recording#h_4cca2458-cbd5-4439-933c-57c0d62a2753).

Only a host or a co-host of the meeting/webinar can start, pause, resume, or stop a [cloud recording](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203741855-Cloud-Recording).

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or higher plan.
* Cloud recording must be enabled on the users' account.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Recording Stopped** subscription must be enabled under the **Recording** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `recording:read:admin`,`recording:write:admin`,`recording:read`,`recording:write`,`recording:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `cloud_recording:read:recording:master`,`cloud_recording:read:recording`,`cloud_recording:read:recording:admin`

**Event type**: `recording.stopped`

 If the recording is of a meeting: 
* 1 — Instant meeting. 
* 2 — Scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A meeting created via PMI (Personal Meeting ID). 
* 7 — A [Personal Audio Conference](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204517069-Getting-Started-with-Personal-Audio-Conference) (PAC). 
* 8 - Recurring meeting with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is of a webinar: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

* 99 — A recording uploaded via the [**Recordings**](https://zoom.us/recording) interface on the Zoom Web Portal.

# recording.batch_trashed

The **All recording files have been deleted to the Trash** event is triggered every time one of your app users or account users temporarily delete all of their cloud recordings.

When a user initially deletes recordings, the recordings are sent to the trash. Users can delete a recording temporarily via the [**Delete meeting recordings**](/api-reference/zoom-api/ma#operation/recordingDelete) API or via the [**Zoom web client**](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205347605-Recording-Management). At this point, recordings can be recovered up to 30 days after deletion.

## Prerequisites

* Pro or a higher plan.
* Cloud recording must be enabled on the users' account.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **All recording files have been deleted to the Trash** subscription must be enabled under the **Recording** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `recording:read:admin`,`recording:write:admin`,`recording:read`,`recording:write`,`recording:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `cloud_recording:read:recording:admin`

**Event type**: `recording.batch_trashed`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who batch deleted recordings to trash.

operator.description: The email address of the user who batch deleted recordings to trash.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who batch deleted recordings to trash.

The type of batch operation: 
* trash_user_recordings — User recordings trashed. 
* trash_account_recordings — Account recordings trashed.

# webinar.started

The **Start Webinar** event is triggered every time a user in your account or your app starts a webinar.

Zoom users with a [Webinar Plan](https://zoom.us/webinar) have permissions for creating and managing webinars. [Zoom Video Webinar](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/200917029-Getting-Started-With-Webinar) allows users to broadcast a Zoom meeting to up to 1000 attendees. A webinar host can [start a webinar](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/200917029-Getting-Started-With-Webinar#h_816c5048-c9d3-4930-894d-af63cd41de3d) from the Zoom Client or from the [Zoom Web Portal](https://zoom.us).

## Prerequisites

* Pro or a higher plan with Webinar license.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Start Webinar** subscription enabled under the **Webinar** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:admin`,`webinar:write:admin`,`webinar:read`,`webinar:write`,`webinar:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:webinar:master`,`webinar:read:webinar`,`webinar:read:webinar:admin`

**Event type**: `webinar.started`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who started the webinar.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

type.description:
The webinar type. 
* 5 - A webinar. 
* 6 - A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 - A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

# meeting.chat_message_file_downloaded
The **In-meeting chat message file has been downloaded** event is triggered when a meeting's message file is downloaded. The event is sent to the configured endpoint in your Marketplace app.

## Prerequisites

* Have Zoom enable the DLP for in-meeting chat feature to use this webhook.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * Enable the **In-meeting chat message file has been downloaded** subscription under the **Meeting** event category.

This feature is **only** enabled upon request approval. Contact the [Zoom support team](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362003) for more information.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:chat_message`,`meeting:read:chat_message:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.chat_message_file_downloaded`

event_ts.description: The timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who downloaded the file. This value returns blank for external users.

operator.description: The email address of the user who downloaded the file. This value returns the operator name for external users.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who downloaded the file. This value returns blank for external users.

object.description: Information about the chat message file.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

host_account_id.description: The meeting host's account ID. The value will be an empty string if this info is from an external user.

chat_message_file.description: Information about the chat message file.

file_id.description: The live meeting chat file's UUID.

file_name.description: The live meeting chat file's name.

file_size.description: The live meeting chat file's size, in bytes.

file_type.description: The live meeting chat file's file type.

file_owner_id.description: The user ID of the user who uploaded the team chat file. This value returns blank for external users.

event_ts.description: The timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who downloaded the file. This value returns blank for external users.

operator.description: The email address of the user who downloaded the file. This value returns the operator name for external users.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who downloaded the file. This value returns blank for external users.

object.description: Information about the chat message file.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

host_account_id.description: The meeting host's account ID. The value will be an empty string if this info is from an external user.

file_id.description: The live meeting chat file's UUID.

file_name.description: The live meeting chat file's name.

file_type.description: The live meeting chat file's file type.

file_owner_id.description: The user ID of the user who uploaded the team chat file. This value returns blank for external users.

# webinar.sharing_ended

The **Webinar Sharing Ended** event is triggered every time one of your app users or account users stop sharing their screen during a Webinar.

Zoom supports [screen sharing](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362153-Screen-Sharing-and-Annotations) by host or panelists on desktop, tablet and mobile devices running Zoom during webinars.

## Prerequisites

* Pro or a higher plan with Webinar license.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Webinar Sharing Ended** subscription enabled under the **Webinar** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:admin`,`webinar:write:admin`,`webinar:read`,`webinar:write`,`webinar:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:participant_sharing:master`,`webinar:read:participant_sharing`,`webinar:read:participant_sharing:admin`

**Event type**: `webinar.sharing_ended`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the webinar.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

type.description:
The webinar type: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

user_id.description: The participant's webinar user ID. This value is assigned to a participant upon joining a webinar and is only valid for the duration of the webinar.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID): 
* If the participant joins the webinar by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the webinar **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

content.description:
The type of shared content: 
* application — An application. For example, a web browser. 
* whiteboard — The [classic whiteboard](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205677665-Sharing-a-whiteboard). 
* desktop — A user's desktop. 
* airplay — A user's [AirPlay (Apple screen mirroring)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204726885-Sharing-screen-using-Apple-screen-mirroring-AirPlay-). 
* camera — A user's camera. 
* unknown — An unrecognized application, such as a third party app.

link_source.description:
The Dropbox sharing method: 
* deep_link — A [deep link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_linking). 
* in_meeting — A link shared via Zoom. 
* An empty string value if the link source is **not** a Dropbox link.

file_link.description: The Dropbox file's link. This field returns an empty string value if the file link is **not** from a Dropbox source.

date_time.description: The screenshare's date and time.

source.description:
The source from which the file was shared: 
* dropbox — A Dropbox source. 
* An empty string if the source is **not** Dropbox.

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the webinar.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

type.description:
The webinar type: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

user_id.description: The participant's webinar user ID. This value is assigned to a participant upon joining a webinar and is only valid for the duration of the webinar.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID): 
* If the participant joins the webinar by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the webinar **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

content.description:
The type of shared content: 
* application — An application. For example, a web browser. 
* whiteboard — The [classic whiteboard](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205677665-Sharing-a-whiteboard). 
* desktop — A user's desktop. 
* airplay — A user's [AirPlay (Apple screen mirroring)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204726885-Sharing-screen-using-Apple-screen-mirroring-AirPlay-). 
* camera — A user's camera. 
* unknown — An unrecognized application, such as a third party app.

link_source.description:
The Dropbox sharing method: 
* deep_link — A [deep link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_linking). 
* in_meeting — A link shared via Zoom. 
* An empty string value if the link source is **not** a Dropbox link.

file_link.description: The Dropbox file's link. This field returns an empty string value if the file link is **not** from a Dropbox source.

date_time.description: The screenshare's date and time.

source.description:
The source from which the file was shared: 
* dropbox — A Dropbox source. 
* An empty string if the source is **not** Dropbox.

# meeting.summary_trashed

The **Meeting summary has been deleted to trash** event is triggered every time one of your app users or account users temporarily deletes a meeting summary.

## Prerequisites

* Host user must be Pro or higher plan.
* Enable the **Meeting Summary with AI Companion** feature in the host's account.
* E2ee meetings do not have summary feature enabled.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Meeting summary has been deleted to trash** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting_summary:read:admin`,`meeting_summary:read`,`meeting_summary:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:summary:master`,`meeting:read:summary`,`meeting:read:summary:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.summary_trashed`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who hosted the meeting.

operator.description: The email address of the user who deleted the meeting summary.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who deleted the meeting summary.

meeting_host_id.description: The ID of the user who is set as the meeting host.

meeting_uuid.description: Unique meeting ID. Each meeting instance generates its own meeting UUID - after a meeting ends, a new UUID is generated for the next instance of the meeting. Retrieve a list of UUIDs from past meeting instances using the [**List past meeting instances**](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/#operation/pastMeetings) API. [Double encode](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#meeting-id-and-uuid) your UUID when using it for API calls if the UUID begins with a / or contains // in it.

meeting_id.description: [Meeting ID](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362373-What-is-a-Meeting-ID-) - the meeting's unique identifier in **long** format, represented as int64 data type in JSON. Also known as the meeting number.

meeting_start_time.description: The meeting's start date and time.

meeting_end_time.description: The meeting's end date and time.

summary_start_time.description: The summary's start date and time.

summary_end_time.description: The summary's end date and time.

summary_created_time.description: The date and time when the meeting summary was created.

summary_last_modified_time.description: The date and time when the meeting summary was last modified.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who hosted the meeting.

operator.description: The email address of the user who deleted the meeting summary.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who deleted the meeting summary.

meeting_host_id.description: The ID of the user who is set as the meeting host.

meeting_uuid.description: Unique meeting ID. Each meeting instance generates its own meeting UUID - after a meeting ends, a new UUID is generated for the next instance of the meeting. Retrieve a list of UUIDs from past meeting instances using the [**List past meeting instances**](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/#operation/pastMeetings) API. [Double encode](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#meeting-id-and-uuid) your UUID when using it for API calls if the UUID begins with a / or contains // in it.

meeting_id.description: [Meeting ID](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362373-What-is-a-Meeting-ID-) - the meeting's unique identifier in **long** format, represented as int64 data type in JSON. Also known as the meeting number.

meeting_start_time.description: The meeting's start date and time.

meeting_end_time.description: The meeting's end date and time.

summary_start_time.description: The summary's start date and time.

summary_end_time.description: The summary's end date and time.

summary_created_time.description: The date and time when the meeting summary was created.

summary_last_modified_time.description: The date and time when the meeting summary was last modified.

requestBody.description:
# meeting.sharing_started

The **Meeting Sharing Started** event is triggered every time an attendee or a host begins sharing their screen during a Zoom meeting. The meeting host must be a user in your account or a user in any other accounts that have installed your [webhook-enabled](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#enable-webhooks) app.

Zoom allows [screen sharing](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362153-Screen-Sharing-and-Annotations) by host and attendees on desktop, tablet, and mobile devices running Zoom during meetings.

## Prerequisites

* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Meeting Sharing Started** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant_sharing:master`,`meeting:read:participant_sharing`,`meeting:read:participant_sharing:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.sharing_started`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

user_id.description: The participant's meeting user ID. This value is assigned to a participant upon joining a meeting and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID): 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

content.description:
The type of shared content: 
* application — An application. For example, a web browser. 
* whiteboard — The [classic whiteboard](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205677665-Sharing-a-whiteboard). 
* desktop — A user's desktop. 
* airplay — A user's [AirPlay (Apple screen mirroring)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204726885-Sharing-screen-using-Apple-screen-mirroring-AirPlay-). 
* camera — A user's camera. 
* unknown — An unrecognized application, such as a third party app.

link_source.description:
The Dropbox sharing method: 
* deep_link — A [deep link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_linking). 
* in_meeting — A link shared via Zoom. 
* An empty (””) value if the link source is **not** a Dropbox link.

file_link.description: The Dropbox file's link. This field returns an empty (””) value if the file link is **not** from a Dropbox source.

date_time.description: The screenshare's date and time.

source.description:
The source from which the file was shared: 
* dropbox — A Dropbox source. 
* An empty (””) value if the source is **not** Dropbox.

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

user_id.description: The participant's meeting user ID. This value is assigned to a participant upon joining a meeting and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID): 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

content.description:

* application — An application. For example, a web browser. 
* whiteboard — The [classic whiteboard](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205677665-Sharing-a-whiteboard). 
* desktop — A user's desktop. 
* airplay — A user's [AirPlay (Apple screen mirroring)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204726885-Sharing-screen-using-Apple-screen-mirroring-AirPlay-). 
* camera — A user's camera. 
* unknown — An unrecognized application, such as a third party app.

link_source.description:
The Dropbox sharing method: 
* deep_link — A [deep link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_linking). 
* in_meeting — A link shared via Zoom. 
* An empty (””) value if the link source is **not** a Dropbox link.

file_link.description: The Dropbox file's link. This field returns an empty (””) value if the file link is **not** from a Dropbox source.

date_time.description: The screenshare's date and time.

source.description:
The source from which the file was shared: 
* dropbox — A Dropbox source. 
* An empty (””) value if the source is **not** Dropbox.
