# webinar.registration_created

The **Webinar Registration has been created** event is triggered every time someone registers for a Webinar created by one of your app users or account users.

Zoom users with a [Webinar Plan](https://zoom.us/webinar) have access to creating and managing Webinars. [Zoom Video Webinar](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/200917029-Getting-Started-With-Webinar) allows users to broadcast a Zoom meeting to up to 1000 attendees. [Scheduling a Webinar with registration](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204619915-Scheduling-a-Webinar-with-Registration) requires your registrants to complete a brief form before receiving the link to join the Webinar.

## Prerequisites

* Pro or a higher plan with Webinar license.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace App](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event notification endpoint URL**
	* The **Webinar Registration has been created** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:admin`,`webinar:write:admin`,`webinar:read`,`webinar:write`,`webinar:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:registrant:master`,`webinar:read:registrant`,`webinar:read:registrant:admin`

**Event type**: `webinar.registration_created`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the webinar.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

type.description:
The webinar type: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

occurrences.description: Information about recurring webinar with fixed times and their occurrences. This only returns when the webinar's type value is 9.

zip.description: The registrant's ZIP or postal code.

purchasing_time_frame.description:
The registrant's purchasing time frame: 
* Within a month 
* 1-3 months 
* 4-6 months 
* More than 6 months 
* No timeframe

role_in_purchase_process.description:
The registrant's role in the purchase process: 
* Decision Maker 
* Evaluator/Recommender 
* Influencer 
* Not involved

title.description: The title of the custom question.

value.description: The custom question's response value. This has a limit of 128 characters.

status.description:
The registrant's registration approval status: 
* approve — Approved registrant. 
* pending — Registration approval pending.

join_url.description: The URL to join the webinar.

tracking_source.description: Information about the source tracking. See [Creating webinar registration tracking links](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360000315683-Creating-webinar-registration-tracking-links) for details.

id.description: Unique Identifier of the tracking source.

source_name.description: Name of the source (platform) where the registration URL was shared.

tracking_url.description: Tracking URL. The URL that was shared for the registration.

# meeting.chat_message_sent

The **In-meeting chat message received** event is triggered when a user sends a public or private chat message during a meeting using the [in-meeting Zoom chat feature](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203650445-Chatting-in-a-Zoom-meeting). The event is sent to the configured endpoint in your Marketplace app.

## Prerequisites

* Enable the **Enable in-meeting chat DLP (Data Loss Prevention) integration** feature in the host's account.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **In-meeting chat message received** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.
* This feature is **only** enabled upon request approval. Contact the [Zoom support team](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/contact?id=contact_us) for more information.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:chat_message:master`,`meeting:read:chat_message`,`meeting:read:chat_message:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.chat_message_sent`

event_ts.description: The timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

breakout_room_uuid.description: The breakout room's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each breakout room instance generates a breakout room UUID.

date_time.description: The date and time when the chat message was sent.

sender_type.description:
The type of user that sent the message: 
* host — The meeting host. 
* guest — A meeting guest.

sender_context.description:
The context where the sender is located when the message is generated. 
* meeting - The sender is in a meeting. 
* breakout_room - The sender is in a breakout room. 
* waiting_room - The sender is in the waiting room.

recipient_email.description: The message recipient's email address. Optional only included if it is a private message.

recipient_type.description:
The type of message recipient. 
* everyone - All meeting participants. 
* host - The meeting host. 
* guest - A meeting guest. 
* group - A group chat. 
* host_group - The meeting host and all co-hosts.

recipient_context.description:
The context where the recipient is located when the message is delivered. 
* meeting - The recipient is in the meeting. 
* breakout_room - The recipient is in a breakout room. 
* waiting_room - The recipient is in the waiting room.

file_ids.description: The live webinar chat file's UUID, in base64-encoded format.

json-example.value:
{
  "event": "meeting.chat_message_sent",
  "event_ts": 1626230691572,
  "payload": {
    "account_id": "AAAAAABBBB",
    "object": {
      "id": 1234567890,
      "uuid": "4444AAAiAAAAAiAiAiiAii==",
      "breakout_room_uuid": "FkQbpP2UR028mDrwzEahqw==",
      "chat_message": {
        "date_time": "GMT20220110-072010",
        "sender_session_id": "26219520",
        "sender_name": "Jill Chill",
        "sender_email": "dlp.user@example.com",
        "sender_type": "host",
        "sender_context": "meeting",
        "recipient_session_id": "38681600",
        "recipient_name": "John Smith",
        "recipient_email": "guest.user@example",
        "recipient_type": "guest",
        "recipient_context": "waiting_room",
        "message_id": "MS17MDQ5NjE4QjYtRjk4Ny00REEwLUFBQUItMTg3QTY0RjU2MzhFfQ==",
        "message_content": "This is a test message",
        "file_ids": [
          "MS17RDk0QTY3QUQtQkFGQy04QTJFLTI2RUEtNkYxQjRBRTU1MTk5fQ==",
          "MS17NDQ0OEU5MjMtM0JFOS1CMDA1LTQ0NDAtQjdGOTU0Rjk5MTkyfQ=="
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}

event_ts.description: The timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

breakout_room_uuid.description: The breakout room's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each breakout room instance generates a breakout room UUID.

date_time.description: The date and time when the chat message was sent.

sender_type.description:
The type of user that sent the message: 
* host — The meeting host. 
* guest — A meeting guest.

sender_context.description:
The context where the sender is located when the message is generated. 
* meeting - The sender is in a meeting. 
* breakout_room - The sender is in a breakout room. 
* waiting_room - The sender is in the waiting room.

recipient_email.description: The message recipient's email address. Optional only included if it is a private message.

recipient_type.description:
The type of message recipient. 
* everyone - All meeting participants. 
* host - The meeting host. 
* guest - A meeting guest. 
* group - A group chat. 
* host_group - The meeting host and all co-hosts.

recipient_context.description:
The context where the recipient is located when the message is delivered. 
* meeting - The recipient is in the meeting. 
* breakout_room - The recipient is in a breakout room. 
* waiting_room - The recipient is in the waiting room.

file_ids.description: The live webinar chat file's UUID, in base64-encoded format.

json-example.value:
{
  "event": "meeting.chat_message_sent",
  "event_ts": 1626230691572,
  "payload": {
    "account_id": "AAAAAABBBB",
    "object": {
      "id": 1234567890,
      "uuid": "4444AAAiAAAAAiAiAiiAii==",
      "breakout_room_uuid": "FkQbpP2UR028mDrwzEahqw==",
      "chat_message": {
        "date_time": "GMT20220110-072010",
        "sender_session_id": "26219520",
        "sender_name": "Jill Chill",
        "sender_email": "dlp.user@example.com",
        "sender_type": "host",
        "sender_context": "meeting",
        "recipient_session_id": "38681600",
        "recipient_name": "John Smith",
        "recipient_email": "guest.user@example",
        "recipient_type": "guest",
        "recipient_context": "waiting_room",
        "message_id": "MS17MDQ5NjE4QjYtRjk4Ny00REEwLUFBQUItMTg3QTY0RjU2MzhFfQ==",
        "message_content": "This is a test message",
        "file_ids": [
          "MS17RDk0QTY3QUQtQkFGQy04QTJFLTI2RUEtNkYxQjRBRTU1MTk5fQ==",

        ]
      }
    }
  }
}

requestBody.description:
# meeting.invitation_rejected

The **Invited party rejected a meeting invite** event is triggered every time a user sends an invitation to join a meeting or video call to one or more recipients.

This event is only suitable for triggering with the ZOOM client.

## Prerequisites

* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with these configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Invited party rejected a meeting invite** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:invitation`,`meeting:read:invitation:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.invitation_rejected`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The meeting host's account ID.

operator.description: The email address of the user who rejected the meeting.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who rejected the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

participant_user_id.description: The user ID of the user who received the meeting invitation.

email.description: The email address of the user who received the meeting invitation. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, the email field returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. For more information, read our [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) documentation.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The meeting host's account ID.

operator.description: The email address of the user who rejected the meeting.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who rejected the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

participant_user_id.description: The user ID of the user who received the meeting invitation.

email.description: The email address of the user who received the meeting invitation. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, the email field returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. For more information, read our [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) documentation.

requestBody.description:
# meeting.participant_role_changed

The **Participant role changed** event is triggered every time a host or meeting attendee changes their [role](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360040324512-Roles-in-a-meeting) during the meeting. A meeting host must be a user in your account or a user in any other accounts that have installed your [webhook-enabled](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#enable-webhooks) app.

## Prerequisites

* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Participant role changed** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant:master`,`meeting:read:participant`,`meeting:read:participant:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.participant_role_changed`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (personal audio conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

user_id.description: The participant's meeting user ID. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

email.description: The participant's email address. This response only returns if the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, this returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. For more information, read our [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) documentation.

registrant_id.description: The participant's registrant ID. A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026).

participant_user_id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID).
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

This value returns blank for external users.

participant_uuid.description: The participant's UUID for this specific meeting and any breakout rooms created in this meeting. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of that meeting.

date_time.description: The time at which the participant changed the [role](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360040324512-Roles-in-a-meeting).

old_role.description:
The participant's previous role: 
* host — Host. 
* co-host — Co-host. 
* attendee — Attendee.

new_role.description:
The participant's new role: 
* host — Host. 
* co-host — Co-host. 
* attendee — Attendee.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (personal audio conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

email.description: The participant's email address. This response only returns if the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, this returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. For more information, read our [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) documentation.

registrant_id.description: The participant's registrant ID. A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026).

participant_user_id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID).
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

This value returns blank for external users.

participant_uuid.description: The participant's UUID for this specific meeting and any breakout rooms created in this meeting. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of that meeting.

date_time.description: The time at which the participant changed the [role](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360040324512-Roles-in-a-meeting).

old_role.description:
The participant's previous role: 
* host — Host. 
* co-host — Co-host. 
* attendee — Attendee.

new_role.description:
The participant's new role: 
* host — Host. 
* co-host — Co-host. 
* attendee — Attendee.

# meeting.permanently_deleted

The **Meeting has been permanently deleted** event is triggered every time a meeting scheduled by one of your app users or account users, is permanently deleted.

A meeting host or a user with admin privileges [permanently delete a meeting](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360048488451-Deleting-local-or-cloud-recordings#h_b35c92a6-ec9e-4025-ab77-f0b52168aed2) via the [Zoom web portal](https://zoom.us). You can also programmatically delete a meet via the [**Delete a meeting**](/docs/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/meetingDelete) API.

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or higher plan.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Meeting has been permanently deleted** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:meeting:master`,`meeting:read:meeting`,`meeting:read:meeting:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.permanently_deleted`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who permanently deleted the meeting.

operator.description: The email address of the user who permanently deleted the meeting.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who permanently deleted the meeting.

operation.description:
The type of operation performed: 
* all — The change was applied to all meetings. 
* single — The change was applied only to a single meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

occurrences.description: Information about recurring meetings with fixed times and their occurrences. This only returns when the meeting's type value is 8.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who permanently deleted the meeting.

operator.description: The email address of the user who permanently deleted the meeting.

* single — The change was applied only to a single meeting.

* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

occurrences.description: Information about recurring meetings with fixed times and their occurrences. This only returns when the meeting's type value is 8.

# recording.registration_created

The **View registrant has registered for this recording** event is triggered every time a user registers for an on-demand cloud recording.

[Cloud recordings](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203741855-Cloud-Recording) of Zoom meetings and webinars can be made [on-demand](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360000488283-On-demand-Recordings). With on-demand recordings, users can view the recordings of past meetings and webinars at any time as long as they have not been removed. To view cloud recordings, users can register manually by filling out the registration form that pops up when users click on the **Recording URL**.

Users can also be programmatically registered via the [**Create a recording registrant**](/api-reference/zoom-api/ma#operation/meetingRecordingRegistrantCreate) API.
Once they complete the registration they can view the recording.

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or higher plan.
* Cloud recording must be enabled on the users' account.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **View registrant has registered for this recording** subscription enabled under the **Recording** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `recording:read:admin`,`recording:write:admin`,`recording:read`,`recording:write`,`recording:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `cloud_recording:read:registrant:master`,`cloud_recording:read:registrant`,`cloud_recording:read:registrant:admin`

**Event type**: `recording.registration_created`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the host that recorded the meeting or webinar.

id.description: The ID of the recorded meeting (meetingId) or webinar (webinarId).

uuid.description: The universally unique identifier (UUID) of the recorded meeting or webinar instance.

host_id.description: The webinar or meeting host's user ID.

type.description:
The type of recorded meeting or webinar: 

 If the recording is of a meeting: 
* 1 — Instant meeting. 
* 2 — Scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A meeting created via PMI (Personal Meeting ID). 
* 7 — A [Personal Audio Conference](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204517069-Getting-Started-with-Personal-Audio-Conference) (PAC). 
* 8 - Recurring meeting with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is of a webinar: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is **not** from a meeting or webinar: 

* 99 — A recording uploaded via the [**Recordings**](https://zoom.us/recording) interface on the Zoom Web Portal.

start_time.description: The meeting or webinar's start time.

duration.description: The scheduled meeting or webinar duration.

status.description:
The registrant's status: 
* approved — Registrant is approved. 
* denied — Registrant is denied. 
* pending — Registrant is waiting for approval. 
* all — The default status code.

zip.description: The registrant's ZIP or postal code.

purchasing_time_frame.description:
The registrant's purchasing time frame: 
* Within a month 
* 1-3 months 
* 4-6 months 
* More than 6 months 
* No timeframe

role_in_purchase_process.description:
The registrant's role in the purchase process: 
* Decision Maker 
* Evaluator/Recommender 
* Influencer 
* Not involved

title.description: The title of the custom question.

value.description: The custom question's response value. This has a limit of 128 characters.

json-example.value:
{
  "event": "recording.registration_created",
  "event_ts": 1626230691572,
  "payload": {
    "account_id": "AAAAAABBBB",
    "object": {
      "id": 1234567890,
      "uuid": "4444AAAiAAAAAiAiAiiAii==",
      "host_id": "x1yCzABCDEfg23HiJKl4mN",
      "topic": "My Personal Recording",
      "type": 4,
      "start_time": "2021-07-13T21:44:51Z",
      "timezone": "America/Los_Angeles",
      "duration": 60,
      "occurrences": {
        "occurrence_id": "ABCDE12345",
        "start_time": "2021-07-13T21:44:51Z"
      },
      "registrant": {
        "id": "iFxeBPYun6SAiWUzBcEkX",
        "email": "jchill@example.com",
        "first_name": "Jill",
        "last_name": "Chill",

        "role_in_purchase_process": "Influencer",

        "custom_questions": [
          {
            "title": "What do you hope to learn from this Webinar?",
            "value": "Look forward to learning how you come up with new recipes and what other services you offer."
          }
        ],

      }
    }
  }
}

# meeting.created

The **Meeting has been created** event is triggered every time a meeting is created by one of your app users or account users.

Zoom offers multiple methods for users to schedule new meetings. Users can manually create meetings through the Zoom client or the [Zoom web portal](https://zoom.us/). Users can also programmatically create meetings through the [**Create a meeting**](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/#operation/meetingCreate) API.

## Prerequisites

Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
* The **Meeting has been created** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.





**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:meeting:master`,`meeting:read:meeting`,`meeting:read:meeting:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.created`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

operator.description: The email address of the user who created the meeting.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who created the meeting.

operation.description:
The type of operation performed. 
* all - The change was applied to all meetings. 
* single - The change was applied only to a single meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time. 
* 10 - A screen share only meeting.

join_url.description: The URL to join the meeting.

password.description: The password required to join the meeting.

occurrences.description: Information about recurring meetings with fixed times and their occurrences. This only returns when the meeting's type value is 8.

status.description:
The meeting occurrence status: 
* available 
* deleted

use_pmi.description: Whether the use of PMI is enabled.

alternative_hosts.description: A semicolon-separated list of the meeting's alternative hosts' email addresses or IDs.

meeting_invitees.description: A list of the meeting's invitees.

join_before_host.description:
Whether participants can join the meeting before its host. This field is only used for scheduled meetings (2) or recurring meetings (3 and 8). This value defaults to false.

If the [**Waiting Room** feature](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115000332726-Waiting-Room) is enabled, this setting is **disabled**.

jbh_time.description:
If the value of the join_before_host field is true, this field indicates the time limits when a participant can join a meeting before the meeting's host.

* 0 - Allow the participant to join the meeting at anytime.
* 5 - Allow the participant to join 5 minutes before the meeting's start time.
* 10 - Allow the participant to join 10 minutes before the meeting's start time.
* 15 - Allow the participant to join 15 minutes before the meeting's start time.

type.description:
The type of recurring meeting. 
* 1 - Daily. 
* 2 - Weekly. 
* 3 - Monthly.

repeat_interval.description:
The meeting's repeat interval. 
* For daily intervals, a max of 99 days. 
* For a weekly interval, a max of 50 weeks. 
* For monthly intervals, a max of 10 months.

weekly_days.description:
A comma-separated list of the days of the week when the recurring weekly meeting occurs. 
* 1 - Sunday 
* 2 - Monday 
* 3 - Tuesday 
* 4 - Wednesday 
* 5 - Thursday 
* 6 - Friday 
* 7 - Saturday

monthly_day.description: The day of the month when the recurring monthly meeting occurs. The value ranges from 1 to 31.

monthly_week_day.description:
The day of the week when the recurring monthly meeting is scheduled. 
* 1 - Sunday 
* 2 - Monday 
* 3 - Tuesday 
* 4 - Wednesday 
* 5 - Thursday 
* 6 - Friday 
* 7 - Saturday

end_times.description: The number of times the recurring meeting will occur before it is canceled.

end_date_time.description: The recurring meeting's final ending date and time before it is canceled, in UTC format.

monthly_week.description:
The week of the month when a recurring meeting occurs. 
* -1 - The last week of the month. 
* 1 - The first week. 
* 2 - The second week. 
* 3 - The third week. 
* 4 - The fourth week.

visible.description: Whether the tracking field is visible in the meeting scheduling options in the Zoom web portal. When the tracking field is disabled, the field can be only be set by users via API.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

operator.description: The email address of the user who created the meeting.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who created the meeting.

operation.description:
The type of operation performed. 
* all - The change was applied to all meetings. 
* single - The change was applied only to a single meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time. 
* 10 - A screen share only meeting.

join_url.description: The URL to join the meeting.

password.description: The password required to join the meeting.

occurrences.description: Information about recurring meetings with fixed times and their occurrences. This only returns when the meeting's type value is 8.

status.description:
The meeting occurrence status: 
* available 
* deleted

use_pmi.description: Whether the use of PMI is enabled.

alternative_hosts.description: A semicolon-separated list of the meeting's alternative hosts' email addresses or IDs.

meeting_invitees.description: A list of the meeting's invitees.

join_before_host.description:
Whether participants can join the meeting before its host. This field is only used for scheduled meetings (2) or recurring meetings (3 and 8). This value defaults to false.

If the [**Waiting Room** feature](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115000332726-Waiting-Room) is enabled, this setting is **disabled**.

jbh_time.description:
If the value of the join_before_host field is true, this field indicates the time limits when a participant can join a meeting before the meeting's host.

* 0 - Allow the participant to join the meeting at anytime.
* 5 - Allow the participant to join 5 minutes before the meeting's start time.
* 10 - Allow the participant to join 10 minutes before the meeting's start time.
* 15 - Allow the participant to join 15 minutes before the meeting's start time.

type.description:
The type of recurring meeting. 
* 1 - Daily. 
* 2 - Weekly. 
* 3 - Monthly.

repeat_interval.description:
The meeting's repeat interval. 
* For daily intervals, a max of 99 days. 
* For a weekly interval, a max of 50 weeks. 
* For monthly intervals, a max of 10 months.

weekly_days.description:
A comma-separated list of the days of the week when the recurring weekly meeting occurs. 
* 1 - Sunday 
* 2 - Monday 
* 3 - Tuesday 
* 4 - Wednesday 
* 5 - Thursday 
* 6 - Friday 
* 7 - Saturday

monthly_day.description: The day of the month when the recurring monthly meeting occurs. The value ranges from 1 to 31.

monthly_week_day.description:
The day of the week when the recurring monthly meeting is scheduled. 
* 1 - Sunday 
* 2 - Monday 
* 3 - Tuesday 
* 4 - Wednesday 
* 5 - Thursday 
* 6 - Friday 
* 7 - Saturday

end_times.description: The number of times the recurring meeting will occur before it is canceled.

end_date_time.description: The recurring meeting's final ending date and time before it is canceled, in UTC format.

monthly_week.description:
The week of the month when a recurring meeting occurs. 
* -1 - The last week of the month. 
* 1 - The first week. 
* 2 - The second week. 
* 3 - The third week. 
* 4 - The fourth week.

visible.description: Whether the tracking field is visible in the meeting scheduling options in the Zoom web portal. When the tracking field is disabled, the field can be only be set by users via API.

# meeting.live_streaming_started

The **Meeting Live Streaming Started** event is triggered every time a meeting's live stream begins on a streaming platform. The host that begins the live stream must be a user in your account or a user in any other accounts that have installed your [webhook-enabled](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#enable-webhooks) app.

Zoom lets users live stream their Zoom meetings on [YouTube Live](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360028478292-Streaming-a-Meeting-or-Webinar-on-YouTube-Live), [Facebook](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115000350406-Streaming-a-meeting-or-webinar-on-Facebook-Live), [Workplace by Facebook](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115005460286-Live-Stream-Meetings-or-Webinars-on-Workplace-by-Facebook), and via a [custom live streaming services](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115001777826-Live-Streaming-Meetings-or-Webinars-Using-a-Custom-Service).

## Prerequisites

* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Meeting Live Streaming Started** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:livestream:master`,`meeting:read:livestream`,`meeting:read:livestream:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.live_streaming_started`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

operator.description: The email address of the user who updated the meeting.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who updated the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

service.description:
The name of the live streaming service: 
* Facebook 
* Workplace_by_Facebook 
* YouTube 
* Twitch 
* Custom_Live_Streaming_Service

custom_live_streaming_settings.description: Information about the custom live stream.

resolution.description: The number of pixels in each dimension that the video camera can display.

date_time.description: The date and time the live stream started.

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

operator.description: The email address of the user who updated the meeting.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who updated the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

service.description:
The name of the live streaming service: 
* Facebook 
* Workplace_by_Facebook 
* YouTube 
* Twitch 
* Custom_Live_Streaming_Service

custom_live_streaming_settings.description: Information about the custom live stream.

resolution.description: The number of pixels in each dimension that the video camera can display.

date_time.description: The date and time the live stream started.

# meeting.participant_room_system_callout_accepted

The **Invited party answered a meeting invitation through phone (call out) via Zoom room** event is triggered every time a user [joins a meeting through phone (call out) from a Zoom room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205369035).

## Prerequisites

* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
* The **Invited party answered a meeting invitation through phone (call out) via Zoom room** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant_callout:master`,`meeting:read:participant_callout`,`meeting:read:participant_callout:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.participant_room_system_callout_accepted`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the meeting host.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

host_id.description: The user ID of the meeting host.

inviter_name.description: The user name of the event's trigger.

call_type.description: The type of call out. Use a value of h323 or sip.

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the meeting host.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

host_id.description: The user ID of the meeting host.

inviter_name.description: The user name of the event's trigger.

call_type.description: The type of call out. Use a value of h323 or sip.

# webinar.registration_approved

The **Webinar Registration has been approved** event is triggered every time one of your app users or account users approve a user's registration.

[Scheduling a Webinar with registration](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204619915-Scheduling-a-Webinar-with-Registration) requires registrants to complete a brief form before receiving the link to join a Webinar. [Webinar Registration options](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/202835649-Webinar-Registration-Customization#h_b74e0e26-5f3f-40d4-a3de-949dbfd85882) can be customized by a Zoom admin or a Webinar host. This customization allows them to choose whether to approve attendees automatically or manually.

When the registration is set to be approved manually, a pending registrant can either be approved or denied from joining the Webinar.

To approve a registrant manually, navigate to the [Webinars](https://zoom.us/webinar/list) page and click on the name of the webinar. Click **Edit** to the right of **Manage Attendees** option, select the registrant and click **Approve**. Alternatively, use the [**Update registrant's status**](/docs/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/webinarRegistrantStatus) API to approve the registrant programmatically.

## Prerequisites

* Pro or a higher plan with Webinar license.
* **Manual Approval** enabled for [Webinar Registrations](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/211579443-Registration-for-Meetings).
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**
	* The **Webinar Registration has been approved** subscription enabled under the **Webinar** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:admin`,`webinar:write:admin`,`webinar:read`,`webinar:write`,`webinar:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `webinar:read:registrant:master`,`webinar:read:registrant`,`webinar:read:registrant:admin`

**Event type**: `webinar.registration_approved`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who approved the registration.

operator.description: The email address of the user who approved the webinar registration.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who approved the webinar registration.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

type.description:
The webinar type: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

occurrences.description: Information about recurring webinar with fixed times and their occurrences. This only returns when the webinar's type value is 9.

join_url.description: The URL to join the webinar.

tracking_source.description: Information about the source tracking. See [Creating webinar registration tracking links](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360000315683-Creating-webinar-registration-tracking-links) for details.

id.description: Unique Identifier of the tracking source.

source_name.description: Name of the source (platform) where the registration URL was shared.

tracking_url.description: Tracking URL. The URL that was shared for the registration.

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who approved the registration.

operator.description: The email address of the user who approved the webinar registration.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who approved the webinar registration.

uuid.description: The webinar's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each webinar instance generates a webinar UUID.

type.description:
The webinar type: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

occurrences.description: Information about recurring webinar with fixed times and their occurrences. This only returns when the webinar's type value is 9.

join_url.description: The URL to join the webinar.

tracking_source.description: Information about the source tracking. See [Creating webinar registration tracking links](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360000315683-Creating-webinar-registration-tracking-links) for details.

# meeting.breakout_room_sharing_started

The **User in breakout room started sharing** event is triggered when a user in a [breakout room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115005769646-Participating-in-breakout-rooms) begins [sharing content](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362153-Sharing-your-screen-or-desktop-on-Zoom), such as their desktop or the [classic whiteboard](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205677665-Sharing-a-whiteboard).

Breakout rooms are sessions that are split off from the main Zoom meeting. They allow the participants to meet in smaller groups and are completely isolated in terms of audio and video from the main session.

## Prerequisites

* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **User in breakout room started sharing** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant_sharing:master`,`meeting:read:participant_sharing`,`meeting:read:participant_sharing:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.breakout_room_sharing_started`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The main meeting host's account ID.

uuid.description: The main meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

breakout_room_uuid.description: The breakout room's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each breakout room instance generates a breakout room UUID.

host_id.description: The main meeting host's user ID.

type.description:
The main meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

user_id.description: The participant's ID. This is a unique ID assigned to the participant upon joining the meeting and is only valid for that meeting.

parent_user_id.description: The participant's main meeting user ID (user_id).

id.description: The participant's user ID. This is the same value used in the [**Users**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#tag/Users) APIs if the participant logged in to join the meeting.

content.description:
The type of shared content: 
* application — An application. For example, a web browser. 
* whiteboard — The [Zoom Whiteboard](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/4410916881421). 
* desktop — A user's desktop. 
* airplay — A user's airplay. 
* camera — A user's camera. 
* unknown — An unrecognized application, such as a third party app.

link_source.description:
The Dropbox sharing method: 
* deep_link — A [deep link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_linking). 
* in_meeting — A link shared via Zoom. 
* An empty string value if the link source is **not** a Dropbox link.

file_link.description: The Dropbox file's link. This field returns an empty string value if the file link is **not** from a Dropbox source.

date_time.description: The screenshare's date and time.

source.description:
The source from which the file was shared: 
* dropbox — A Dropbox source. 
* An empty string value if the source is **not** Dropbox.

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The main meeting host's account ID.

uuid.description: The main meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

breakout_room_uuid.description: The breakout room's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each breakout room instance generates a breakout room UUID.

host_id.description: The main meeting host's user ID.

type.description:
The main meeting type: 
* 0 — A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 — An instant meeting. 
* 2 — A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 — A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 — A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

user_id.description: The participant's ID. This is a unique ID assigned to the participant upon joining the meeting and is only valid for that meeting.

parent_user_id.description: The participant's main meeting user ID (user_id).

id.description: The participant's user ID. This is the same value used in the [**Users**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#tag/Users) APIs if the participant logged in to join the meeting.

content.description:
The type of shared content: 
* application — An application. For example, a web browser. 
* whiteboard — The [Zoom Whiteboard](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/4410916881421). 
* desktop — A user's desktop. 
* airplay — A user's airplay. 
* camera — A user's camera. 
* unknown — An unrecognized application, such as a third party app.

link_source.description:
The Dropbox sharing method: 
* deep_link — A [deep link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_linking). 
* in_meeting — A link shared via Zoom. 
* An empty string value if the link source is **not** a Dropbox link.

file_link.description: The Dropbox file's link. This field returns an empty string value if the file link is **not** from a Dropbox source.

date_time.description: The screenshare's date and time.

source.description:
The source from which the file was shared: 
* dropbox — A Dropbox source. 
* An empty string value if the source is **not** Dropbox.

# meeting.participant_bind
The **Phone User Bind To Participant** event is triggered every time a phone user joins a meeting and binds to an attendee in the meeting.

## Prerequisites

Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Phone User Bind To Partcipant** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.


**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant:master`,`meeting:read:participant`,`meeting:read:participant:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.participant_bind`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

participant.description: Information about the meeting participant. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, email returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

user_id.description: The participant's meeting user ID. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

bind_user_id.description: The particpant's meeting user ID of participant the phone user bind to. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value. 

 This value returns blank for external users.

**Note:** Use the participant_user_id value instead of this value. We will remove this response in a future release.

participant_uuid.description: The participant's UUID for this specific meeting and any breakout rooms created in this meeting. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of that meeting.

bind_participant_uuid.description: The participant's UUID of the user which this phone user binds to.

date_time.description: The time when the participant is bond.

email.description: The participant's email address. This response only returns if the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, this returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

participant_user_id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

 This value returns blank for external users.

registrant_id.description: The registrant ID of the participants this phone user binds to. A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026).

phone_number.description: Phone number of participants who joined via PSTN.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

participant.description: Information about the meeting participant. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, email returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

user_id.description: The participant's meeting user ID. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

bind_user_id.description: The particpant's meeting user ID of participant the phone user bind to. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value. 

 This value returns blank for external users.

**Note:** Use the participant_user_id value instead of this value. We will remove this response in a future release.

participant_uuid.description: The participant's UUID for this specific meeting and any breakout rooms created in this meeting. This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of that meeting.

bind_participant_uuid.description: The participant's UUID of the user which this phone user binds to.

date_time.description: The time when the participant is bond.

email.description: The participant's email address. This response only returns if the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, this returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

participant_user_id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

 This value returns blank for external users.

registrant_id.description: The registrant ID of the participants this phone user binds to. A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026).

phone_number.description: Phone number of participants who joined via PSTN.

# meeting.participant_feedback

The **Get participant survey feedback for meetings** event is triggered every time an attendee completes an [end-of-meeting experience feedback survey](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115005855266-End-of-meeting-experience-feedback-survey) for a meeting. A meeting attendee is a meeting participant or the host.

## Prerequisites

* Enable the **Display end-of-meeting experience feedback survey** feature in the attendee's Settings page.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
  * A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
  * The **Get participant survey feedback for meetings** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.

**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant_feedback:master`,`meeting:read:participant_feedback`,`meeting:read:participant_feedback:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.participant_feedback`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who commits the feedback.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

participant_uuid.description: The participant's meeting universally unique ID (UUID). This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of the meeting. This value will not be changed when the participant joins/leaves a breakout room.

participant_user_id.description: The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). This value returns blank for users who aren't logged into Zoom.

feedback_details.description: The feedback details. This returns null if the satisfied field is true.

id.description:
The feedback ID. 
* 1 — Poor video quality. 
* 2 — Poor audio quality. 
* 5 — Unable to present using share.zoom.us. 
* 7 — Unable to make phone call. 
* 8 — They could not hear us. 
* 9 — We could not hear them. 
* 10 — They could not see us. 
* 11 — We could not see them. 
* 12 — Could not present computer screen. 
* 999 — Others.

comments.description: The participant's comments. This returns null if the satisfied field is true.

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who commits the feedback.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

participant_uuid.description: The participant's meeting universally unique ID (UUID). This value is assigned to a participant when they join a meeting, and is only valid for the duration of the meeting. This value will not be changed when the participant joins/leaves a breakout room.

participant_user_id.description: The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). This value returns blank for users who aren't logged into Zoom.

feedback_details.description: The feedback details. This returns null if the satisfied field is true.

id.description:
The feedback ID. 
* 1 — Poor video quality. 
* 2 — Poor audio quality. 
* 5 — Unable to present using share.zoom.us. 
* 7 — Unable to make phone call. 
* 8 — They could not hear us. 
* 9 — We could not hear them. 
* 10 — They could not see us. 
* 11 — We could not see them. 
* 12 — Could not present computer screen. 
* 999 — Others.

comments.description: The participant's comments. This returns null if the satisfied field is true.

# recording.deleted

This event is triggered when a user permanently deletes a cloud recording.

When a user initially deletes a recording, it is sent to the trash. At this stage, the recording can still be recovered for up to 30 days after deletion. To permanently delete a recording, users must delete it from the trash.

Users can permanently delete a recording via the [**Delete meeting recordings**](/api-reference/zoom-api/ma#operation/recordingDelete) API with the `delete` query parameter or in the [Zoom web portal](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205347605-Recording-Management).

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or higher plan.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Recording files have been permanently deleted** subscription enabled under the **Recording** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `recording:read:admin`,`recording:write:admin`,`recording:read`,`recording:write`,`recording:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `cloud_recording:read:recording:master`,`cloud_recording:read:recording`,`cloud_recording:read:recording:admin`

id.description: The ID of the recorded meeting (meetingId) or webinar (webinarId).

host_id.description: The ID of the user set as the host of the meeting or webinar.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user that completed the meeting or webinar recording.

type.description:
The type of recorded meeting or webinar: 

 If the recording is of a meeting: 
* 1 — Instant meeting. 
* 2 — Scheduled meeting. 
* 3 — A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 — A meeting created via PMI (Personal Meeting ID). 
* 7 — A [Personal Audio Conference](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/204517069-Getting-Started-with-Personal-Audio-Conference) (PAC). 
* 8 - Recurring meeting with a fixed time. 

 If the recording is of a webinar: 
* 5 — A webinar. 
* 6 — A recurring webinar without a fixed time. 
* 9 — A recurring webinar with a fixed time.

file_extension.description: The file extension type of the recording file.

play_url.description: The URL at which the recording file can be opened and played.

download_url.description:
The URL at which to download the the recording. 

 **JWT apps** 

 To access a private or password-protected cloud recording of a user in your account, use a [Zoom JWT app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/getting-started/app-types/create-jwt-app). Use the generated JWT token as the value of the access_token query parameter and include this query parameter at the end of the URL. 

 https://{{base-domain}}/recording/download/{{path-to-file-download}}?access_token={{JWT-token}} 

 **OAuth apps** 

 If a user has authorized and installed your OAuth app that contains recording scopes, use the user's [OAuth access token](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth/) to download the file. For example: 

 https://{{base-domain}}/rec/archive/download/xxx?access_token={{OAuth-access-token}}. 

**Note:** We recommend that you send the access_token as a Bearer token in the Authorization header, for example: "Authorization": "Bearer <ACCESS_TOKEN>”.

# recording.paused

The **Recording Paused** event is triggered every time a recording is paused by one of your app's users or account users.

All users with a Pro or a higher plan have access to [cloud recordings](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203741855-Cloud-Recording). An account owner or account admin can [enable cloud recording](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203741855-Cloud-Recording#h_29fc1846-190d-4f05-ab23-1070c8106ec8) for all account members as well as for members that belong to a certain group. Users can also enable cloud recordings [for their own use](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203741855-Cloud-Recording#h_4cca2458-cbd5-4439-933c-57c0d62a2753).

Only a host or a co-host of the meeting/webinar can start, pause, resume or stop a [cloud recording](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203741855-Cloud-Recording).

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or a higher plan.
* Cloud recording must be enabled on the users' account.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Recording Paused** subscription must be enabled under the **Recording** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `recording:read:admin`,`recording:write:admin`,`recording:read`,`recording:write`,`recording:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `cloud_recording:read:recording:master`,`cloud_recording:read:recording`,`cloud_recording:read:recording:admin`

**Event type**: `recording.paused`

# meeting.participant_room_system_callout_ringing

The **Invited party's phone (call out) ringing in Zoom room** event is triggered every time a user's phone is ringing when they were invited to [join a meeting through phone (call out) from a Zoom room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205369035).

## Prerequisites

* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
* The **Invited party's phone (call out) ringing in Zoom room** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant_callout:master`,`meeting:read:participant_callout`,`meeting:read:participant_callout:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.participant_room_system_callout_ringing`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the meeting host.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

host_id.description: The user ID of the meeting host.

inviter_name.description: The user name of the event's trigger.

call_type.description: The type of call out. Use a value of h323 or sip.

# recording.started

The **Recording Started** event is triggered every time a recording is started by one of your app users or account users.

All users with a Pro or a higher plan have access to [cloud recordings](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203741855-Cloud-Recording). An account owner or account admin can [enable cloud recording](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203741855-Cloud-Recording#h_29fc1846-190d-4f05-ab23-1070c8106ec8) for all account members as well as for members that belong to a certain group. Users can also enable cloud recordings [for their own use](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203741855-Cloud-Recording#h_4cca2458-cbd5-4439-933c-57c0d62a2753).

Only a host or a co-host of the meeting/webinar can [start cloud recording](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/203741855-Cloud-Recording#h_7420acb5-1897-4061-87b4-5b76e99c03b4) of the meeting/webinar.

# user.tsp_updated

The **A user's TSP account was updated** event is triggered every time a [user's TSP account information is updated](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/userTSPUpdate).

Zoom allows certain carrier partners to provision Telephony Service Provider (TSP) audio to users. For users to use TSP audio, carrier partners must create a TSP user account with Zoom for the users.

## Prerequisites

* A Pro or higher plan with TSP Configurations enabled.
* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* The **A user's TSP account was updated** subscription must be enabled under the **TSP** event.



**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `tsp:read:admin`,`tsp:write:admin`,`tsp:read`,`tsp:write`,`tsp:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `tsp:read:tsp_account:master`,`tsp:read:tsp_account`,`tsp:read:tsp_account:admin`

**Event type**: `user.tsp_updated`

operator.description: The email address of the user who updated the TSP.

tsp_credentials.description: Information about the updated user's TSP credentials.

dial_in_numbers.description: Information about the TSP dial-in numbers.

type.description:
The phone number's type. 
* toll - Toll number. 
* tollfree - Toll free number. 
* media_link - Media link.

The TSP country label. 
* US_TSP_TB 
* EU_TSP_TB

# meeting.invitation_timeout

The **Invited party missed a meeting invitation** event is triggered every time an invitation to join a meeting or video call to one or more recipients times out.

This event is only suitable for triggering with the ZOOM client.

## Prerequisites

* **Event Subscriptions** must be enabled for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with the following configurations:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Invited party missed a meeting invitation** subscription enabled under the **Meeting** event.





**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:invitation`,`meeting:read:invitation:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.invitation_timeout`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The meeting host's account ID.

operator.description: The email address of the user who missed the meeting invitation.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who missed the meeting invitation.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

participant_user_id.description: The user ID of the user who received the meeting invitation.

email.description: The email address of the user who received the meeting invitation. participant_user_id.description: The user ID of the user who received the meeting invitation.

email.description: The email address of the user who received the meeting invitation. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, the email field returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. For more information, read our [Email address display rules](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) documentation.

# meeting.participant_admitted

The **Participant was admitted into a meeting** event is triggered every time [a meeting host admits the participant from a waiting room to the meeting](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115000332726-Waiting-Room#h_f493a86f-7d08-4e3b-9d6d-9b236fe9cdcd). The meeting host must be a user in your account or a user in any other accounts that have installed your [webhook-enabled](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/webhook-reference#enable-webhooks) app.

When a participant joins a [waiting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115000332726-Waiting-Room), the meeting host is notified in the Zoom client. The host has the option to admit the participant to the meeting or remove the participant from the waiting room and the meeting.

## Prerequisites

* Enable the [**waiting room**](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115000332726-Waiting-Room) feature for the meeting.
* Enable **Event Subscriptions** for your [Marketplace app](https://marketplace.zoom.us/user/build) with:
	* A valid **Event Notification Endpoint URL**.
	* The **Participant was admitted into a meeting** subscription must be enabled under the **Meeting** event.





**[Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:admin`,`meeting:write:admin`,`meeting:read`,`meeting:write`,`meeting:master`

**[Granular Scopes](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/):** `meeting:read:participant:master`,`meeting:read:participant`,`meeting:read:participant:admin`

**Event type**: `meeting.participant_admitted`

event_ts.description: A timestamp when the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who created the meeting.

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

type.description:
The meeting type. 
* 0 - A prescheduled meeting. 
* 1 - An instant meeting. 
* 2 - A scheduled meeting. 
* 3 - A recurring meeting with no fixed time. 
* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

participant.description: Information about the meeting participant. If the participant is not part of the host's account, email returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#email-address-display-rules) for details.

user_id.description: The participant's meeting user ID. This value is assigned to a participant upon joining a meeting and is only valid for the duration of the meeting.

id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty (””) value.

participant_uuid.description: The participant's meeting universally unique ID (UUID). This value is assigned to a participant upon joining a meeting and is only valid for the duration of the meeting. This value will not be changed when the participant joins/leaves a breakout room.

participant_user_id.description:
The participant's universally unique ID (UUID). 
* If the participant joins the meeting by logging into Zoom, this value is the id value in the [**Get a user**](/api-reference/zoom-api/methods#operation/user) API response. 
* If the participant joins the meeting **without** logging into Zoom, this returns an empty string value.

 This value returns blank for external users.

phone_number.description: Phone number of participant joined via PSTN.

date_time.description: The time when the participant joined the meeting.

email.description: The participant's email address. This response only returns if the participant joined the meeting by logging into Zoom. If the participant is **not** part of the host's account, this returns an empty string value, with some exceptions. See [Email address display rules](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/using-zoom-apis#email-address) for details.

customer_key.description: The participant's SDK identifier. This value can be alphanumeric.

registrant_id.description: The participant's registrant ID. A host or a user with administrative permissions can require [registration for Zoom meetings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026).

uuid.description: The meeting's universally unique identifier (UUID). Each meeting instance generates a meeting UUID.

* 4 - A [personal meeting room](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362843). 
* 7 - A [PAC (Personal Audio Conference)](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/205172455-Hosting-a-Personal-Audio-Conference-PAC-meeting) meeting. 
* 8 - A recurring meeting with a fixed time.

 This value returns blank for external users.

See [Email address display rules](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/api-reference/using-zoom-apis#email-address) for details.
